LDSylvr 11/04/2025 2:40 AM
No. Each month you get the following to give away:
  • 6x Serpent Lies Sails
  • 6x (Total) Gilded Phoenix Ship Set and/or Gilded Phoenix Weapon Set And then every new Season you get:
  • 5x Plunder Pass I’m pretty sure that’s what it is. I’m not at the computer right now.

@Sonicbob (Drew) apologies for the ping, but I am currently editing a 30 minute highlight into a shorter video showing a series of bugs that I had on stream last night with Viiraxe and SwoonMC (Viiraxe and svvoon on SoT). We had over 20 different bugs happen in quick succession, and while it was an absolutely hilarious half hour, it is definitely something that needs attention. I should have it posted in the ticket system in the next hour or so. This one is pretty major, and outlines almost every single bug I have ever heard of or seen in one drawn out series of events.

Almost at May now but I thought i'd share this. Just after Christmas I was asked to make a vid of all the good work people did over the Holidays Hauls event. The Vid never got used but I thought you guys should see it because everyone that took part did a bang up job and you can tell it had a very positive impact on individuals playing the game. Thanks to everyone that provided footage for it, and really just sharing this to say thanks for all your hard work and the happyness you guys bring people by playing the game we love (most of the time ). https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VhOUwamoXwI (edited)

NessieDoes 29/04/2025 7:26 PM
https://x.com/nessiedoes/status/1917299386293576072?s=46&t=lO5BVseg_bu1gr704TURMQ
NessieDoes (@NessieDoes) on X
Please read — I feel that it’s important For ~24 hours, almost all banned #SeaOfThieves players were able to access the game. In the two times that @SeaOfThieves has mentioned their second chance plan, they used specific wording that makes it clear only some accounts will
X • 29/04/2025 7:26 PM
7:27 PM
While kiwi beards are happening I’d love if anyone shares their thoughts on this here
7:29 PM
Been thinking about it since the recent update came out
World of Chap 29/04/2025 7:51 PM
I'm with you on that Nessie. I was suggesting something like that on stream a while back and it felt a little out of pocket to say, but my feelings were similar to yours now. There must be countless banned accounts and people who have continued to ban evade and act up out of spite. A blank slate may allow even the most dejected players a chance to come around and change. I know that Rare will not do this. They have obviously spent a lot of time and effort moderating and banning players. They won't just waste all of that. But I do hope these second chances are given broadly at the very least, I think it's going to be a really great thing for the game and the community. I've just really hated seeing passionate players banned for seemingly obscure or unjust reasons over the years.
2

Anyone that has ever used a cheating software shouldn't get a second chance imo
Amnesya 29/04/2025 8:51 PM
For my part, I know people who were redbeard a very, very long time ago for cheating but who have changed a lot today, they are no longer the same as they were years ago, but these are only words, how can I prove that it is true to a Sea of ​​Thieves moderator...
Puff 29/04/2025 8:56 PM
I'm agreeing with Toxie. I've seen and experienced enough where I never wish for them to set sail in this game again.
NessieDoes 29/04/2025 8:59 PM
But they do continue to play the game on new accounts It sucks and I don’t understand how some people can bring themselves to say those sorts of things, but I would rather that they have an account that they don’t want to lose, which could stop them from saying those things over and over again on alts
𝖙𝖔𝖝𝖎𝖊 𝖘𝖎𝖓𝖈𝖑𝖆𝖎𝖗𝖊 29/04/2025 9:01 PM
i dont think everyone that gets banned is making alt accounts and continuing to play
NessieDoes 29/04/2025 9:02 PM
I agree, and I think it would be very unlikely for players that didn’t make an alt to keep playing, to return if an large unban wave did happen
Puff 29/04/2025 9:04 PM
Might sound petty but bad actions should have their consequences, including not having their main accounts back. I've been the victim to be targeted by alt accounts which have resulted to it just being another "feature" at this point and unfortunately can't counter a better alternative to solve the situation. But at the core, I don't want them back. (edited)
2
9:07 PM
But I do appreciate you bringing the conversation up Nessie, as I know you have good intentions and it might summon new ideas. I just personally don't stand behind this particular one. (edited)

1
NessieDoes 29/04/2025 9:09 PM

1
NessieDoes
They get more than a second chance, by making alts and playing the game over and over and over again. I’d much rather they have an account that they don’t want to lose and stop cheating
2oldRudi 29/04/2025 10:19 PM
If some banned players already play the game again on a new account and behave positively, doesn’t that mean things are already fine as they are? I mean they keep playing it, and don't harm new players because they learned their lessons ? I'm not against the idea. I just want to understand your perspective better. What do you think unbanning them would change? What's the the end purpose ?
Flux 29/04/2025 10:29 PM
A bit of a difficult one really, I get both POVs. People do change and a lot of SOT players are/were very young, I think we can all agree as a 15 yo you sometimes don't make the brightest decisions in life and it's part of growing up. Let's say someone that got banned at that age 5 years ago, they'll be 20 now. Should they really still be punished as adults for something they did as teenagers? Then on the other hand you do unfortunately have people that will never learn and will start misbehaving again. Probably smartest to look at the original reason for the ban > have they evaded the ban > have they misbehaved whilst evading the ban. Probably also smart to consider how long ago the original ban was.
Spammals 30/04/2025 11:23 AM
I'd never get behind any suggestion of a mass unban. if someone feels they were banned for something minor they should contact CS but in terms of "lets un ban everyone and give everyone a 2nd chance" god no.
1
𝕻𝖆𝖓𝖟𝖊𝖗𝖇𝖑𝖎𝖙𝖟 30/04/2025 11:33 AM
There is so many horrible people in the batch, i would never ever open the lid, if they behave with new accounts, good for them. (edited)
HitboTC 30/04/2025 1:58 PM
I am still unable to submit prizes @🚫Back on Wednesday🚫 @H1gh Voltage
1:58 PM
PhuzzyBond 30/04/2025 2:28 PM
I'm usually on the merciful side of things, because people change, and a lot of these cheaters are un-guided basement dwellers of a young age who find these ways to vent in their lives and get a reaction our of people. I agree with a second chance, but the main issue here isn't the second chance, its the way alt accounts work. These cheaters get a kick of "ha! i am banned, but i'm still playing on another" If the consequences of cheating are much more serious. the percentage of these people will significantly drop and they will behave.
🚫Back on Wednesday🚫 30/04/2025 2:48 PM
Appreciate the thoughtful commentary here but I ask that you consider there is careful consideration and input from multiple teams and experts across the board when it comes to the next step in our enforcement overhaul. We're equipped with a lot more information than you might realise, all of which will inform our next steps. While we also understand there are ways around bans, we will continue to act in the best interest of our players and community, and bans will not be removed without careful consideration as to why the player was banned and their actions since.
2oldRudi 30/04/2025 3:17 PM
I think the right way to go is not to unban everybody and just hope that they will behave correctly in the future. It’s building a better and more effective CM-Support system that allows those « falsely » banned players to enter in dialog and be considered case by case if they think their bans were exaggerated or not legitimate.
🚫Back on Wednesday🚫 30/04/2025 3:18 PM
Appeals have always been a thing and the decisions are based on evidence, as showcased by bans being lifted in the past when people appealed.

@Partners @Partner Legends Very sorry for the ping, but I'm having a bit of an emergency here, and I need the input of the veterans here to make the right decision. Spammals 13/05/2025 1:23 AM
Late to the party here but to echo others. Don’t mention it. The last thing you want is to give power to the troll validating their existence. If anyone types something in chat about it. This is good.

Spammals 13/05/2025 8:24 AM
PayPal had a change some months back where it added a home address to an invoice template. So if I were to donate I could request an invoice and get that information. To fix you had to go into the settings and remove said information from the template. (edited)

Spammals 24/05/2025 3:35 PM
I’ve been really vocal about them for a long time and kept getting shot down for being wrong. The term “risk vs reward” keeps getting used as a cover all but the reality is they’re boring. 3 shots and they over heat? This isn’t risk vs reward when the reward to be had is tiny. Sure sweats will find ways to work with it in very specific scenarios but do you think a normal sot gamer is going to do anything meaningful with them 3 shots before it overheats? And its treasure value is only 250g??? What is the point in this item! Stupid item. Stupid design behind it. Art team crushing it as always.

RocketManKian
Does anyone else use Application Audio Capture on OBS? For some weird reason OBS is picking up game sound and other's crew VOIP but is not picking up my own's crew's VOIP. However strangely, I CAN hear my crew and if I enable desktop audio instead of the specific Game application audio it works
BehavingBeardly 25/05/2025 8:42 AM
check windows default communication device - make sure it matches your default output device - windows is using your headset as comms device so only outputting there it sounds like.... or set up a Virtual audio cable, set that as your default comms device, and capture that in OBS (if your on a 1pc set up) Im about to go live but happy to bring the settings up on stream and show you (I do it all the time lol)
BehavingBeardly
check windows default communication device - make sure it matches your default output device - windows is using your headset as comms device so only outputting there it sounds like.... or set up a Virtual audio cable, set that as your default comms device, and capture that in OBS (if your on a 1pc set up) Im about to go live but happy to bring the settings up on stream and show you (I do it all the time lol)

In english it's : "Others can communicate by voice, text, or invitation Decide who you can communicate with using voice and text on Xbox, and who can send you invitations to parties, games, or clubs." Spammals 31/05/2025 10:39 AM
Regarding the Invite Spam crashing attacks. While Rare are working on it and all details have been handed over. In the meantime you might want to block the following accounts as a way to potentially mitigate any attacks while investigations continue. On Friday I was attacked and crashed 2 times. When I looked at the invite notifications there was over 100+ but noticed a lot of the same names were repeating. woodberry798520 smxkedope ddspammingu x probablyN0T AimD M Aa AimD M A aa ManticFall5144 mC B Jin FourSquiree deathDLRspam rockbeatle#4493 eurro2 DDSpammYou Tsunami2caca S7ptVII Cmhplusplus Additionally as stated above, for the time being its advisable to scramble your gamer tags while streaming. There is a setting you can disable to prevent invites but this also removes in game coms which is a deal breaker for me. Luckily I have alt accounts I can swap into and continue unaffected. Have a great weekend ❤️ (edited)
1
2
Spammals

ShockwaveZ3R0 3/06/2025 3:53 AM
Was there any plans to fix the skull burying bug/exploit? If an enemy crew buries the skull and you steal the map and dig it back up it doesn't count as stolen for the commendation. The simple fix is just to make it so the skull can't be buried. A more complex fix, the "ownership" of an item as it pertaining to "stealing" commendation should not be changed by an item being buried, the original crew that first picked up an item should always be the "owner".
1
ShockwaveZ3R0
Was there any plans to fix the skull burying bug/exploit? If an enemy crew buries the skull and you steal the map and dig it back up it doesn't count as stolen for the commendation. The simple fix is just to make it so the skull can't be buried. A more complex fix, the "ownership" of an item as it pertaining to "stealing" commendation should not be changed by an item being buried, the original crew that first picked up an item should always be the "owner".

Bigfrankie22
Has anyone figured out a tried and true method to prevent the spam invite griefing that's rampant right now? Been happening almost everyday. My current fix attempt is going into Microsoft account settings and turning off comms/invites from all non-friends. If that is the only fix, (I'm not sure it is a fix at all), I think it may also kill hot mic comms so would rather not use that method if possible.
LDSylvr 6/06/2025 9:20 PM
Haven't read all the responses yet, but if you go to: https://www.xbox.com/en-US/user/settings/privacy-and-safety and go to the 4th setting for communication. Set that to just Friends and they won't be able to spam invite you any longer. It does come at a cost though. You won't be able to hear any hot mics from other crews, unless you add them as a friend, but at least your stream/game won't be ruined by these shitters until Microsoft can come up with some other way of blocking them.
LDSylvr
Haven't read all the responses yet, but if you go to: https://www.xbox.com/en-US/user/settings/privacy-and-safety and go to the 4th setting for communication. Set that to just Friends and they won't be able to spam invite you any longer. It does come at a cost though. You won't be able to hear any hot mics from other crews, unless you add them as a friend, but at least your stream/game won't be ruined by these shitters until Microsoft can come up with some other way of blocking them.
Bigfrankie22 6/06/2025 9:28 PM
Appreciate you dude! Yeah Microsoft def needs to get that figured out, pretty glaring issue

Spammals 8/06/2025 11:13 AM

Invite Spam Protection Options

• Change Microsoft setting so only friends can invite. (Will loose hot mics) If you don’t want to loose in game coms, then you can… • Block the gamer tags provided last week to mitigate their attack. New names always welcome for the list. https://discord.com/channels/550315686636027908/1357713534829723688/1378322083146240010 • Log out of game bar. I don’t see a need to uninstall. Logging out should be sufficient. • Disable ingame notifications. • Scramble gamer tags. Not a silver bullet but makes things harder. • Have an alt account ready to play on with an unknown name. • You can request a free name change to rename your main Pirate. You can park your current name on an alt account until it’s safe to return it. These are the options available right now to protect yourself and mitigate the problem. I know it’s frustrating and annoying but this is the job and we gotta deal with it. (edited)
4

Ophie 8/06/2025 3:09 PM
"I know it’s frustrating and annoying but this is the job and we gotta deal with it." We are supposed to be advocates and representatives of the game. It would be nice if it felt like we were being advocated for. I get that this is part of the job, but "hey, people are getting doxxed, hey people are being harassed, hey people are destroying servers, hey people are hacking to the point where they are able to render the game unplayable" should not be responded to with "gotta deal with it". I get that a lot of this falls at Microsofts feet, but is this going to get better? There are entire communities (athenaware and others) that have built their entire foundation on how easy it is to access game files and purposefully ruin other people's days. They are constantly finding new ways to do that. Even if there isn't a solution and we are on our own to deal with it, it would be really nice to get some clarity and some timelines on what can/is going to be done.
3:12 PM
The majority of the time that we bring this stuff here to try to get some guidance on what to do, we end up having to sort it out ourselves, and the majority of the response that we get is "just don't talk about it on-stream" So we have to grin and bear it while these guys just run rampant on us and the rest of the community. That's more than frustrating.
3:18 PM
At what point do we just acknowledge that there is a systemic problem in protecting players? Whether its Rare or Microsoft, there is habitual ball-dropping happening, and more often than not, we end up being the face of those failures while being in the awkward position of still trying to find a way to make it work for creation and employment. We need better communication and we need to understand the processes that are happening, because I am getting fatigued and overwhelmed by being targeted every stream, and having the people that trust me enough to join me on a boat getting targeted as well. Even if we sort out this invite thing, they'll find something else. We're putting bandaids on bulletwounds when we need a serious overhaul on player protection and game security. I don't know if that's a Rare thing or a Microsoft thing, but that's an example of the frustrating lack of clarity that I'm talking about. Are we partners or brand assets? If we're brand assets, then sure, we handle it ourselves, but if we're partners, we should be included in these discussions more, because the silence and lack of guidance affects our motivation, our mentality, and our livelihood.
Spammals 8/06/2025 3:33 PM
As far as im aware the invite spam only started 1 week ago and has been reported to Rare. I personally don't expect this to be an instant fix, or even a 1 week fix. In a landscape that's constantly shifting things like this will continue to happen. What matters is the steps you take to reduce how it affects you. That's on us. Thats our job. I got attacked last week. I made a change. It hasnt happened again. "Even if we sort out this invite thing, they'll find something else." indeed. Thats always going to be the case and is never going to change. Its part of the job dealing with situations like this, in my opinion. Yes we have tools and we have access to report things to Rare so they can hopefully do things on their end. But there will always be "The next thing". It happens to us all. For right or wrong ya just gotta deal with it the best you can. And being Partners simply means we play the game and get some benefits. We're not Co-Devs. We're not part of the team. We can give feedback and Rare may share things with us from time to time but there's zero expectation that will happen. At least thats how I see things. (edited)
3:37 PM
Thats just my 2 cents. You have all the information thats available so the power is yours! I hope everyone can find some peace.

LDSylvr 8/06/2025 10:23 PM
Getting a free name change doesn't do anything, just an FYI. You need an entirely new account with a name that has never been shown on stream because here is how they will already know what your "new" name is. I'm a cheater on a new account and I added Beardageddon as a friend because there is no security in me accepting his friends request. Beardageddon is now my ONLY friend. Beardageddon decides to redeem his name change and changes it to StrawberryBunnyFeet645. When I log back onto my cheater account and look at my friends list, it's not going to say Beardageddon. It's going to say StrawberryBunnyFeet645 and they'll know right away who that is. Without giving away too much information, they already have bot accounts for every single one of us for this very scenario. So again, name changing does not work. It might work for a bit, but the moment they log back onto that account and see your name change, they'll update their list again.
1
Beardageddon
Or is it just a case that those of us who have logged out just haven't been hit yet?
LDSylvr 8/06/2025 10:28 PM
It's this right here. Those that have only logged out of GameBar or uninstalled GameBar but have not set their "Others can communicate with voice, text, or invites" to Friends -or- Blocked just haven't been hit or re-hit again. I got a warning today in DM's that an attack was incoming and they weren't able to do anything and it was noted as such. Yesterday, Boxy had GameBar uninstalled but left the communicate set to "Everyone" and he was hit while you and I were sailing together.
Spammals 8/06/2025 10:29 PM
Sure but thats the worst case scenario. Only the most dedicated of trolls eager to hunt you. In most other cases they're too lazy to do that and will move on to another target. It's no silver bullet but Is about mitigating and reducing your risk. Its still worth doing, in my opinion, to protect yourself from the majority of attacks. But yes there's always the chance you have someone truly addicted coming for you.
10:32 PM

LDSylvr 8/06/2025 11:02 PM
What about the website setting?
11:05 PM
This is the current way I know to 100% combat it. They tried hitting me today with it, because I got a pre-warning of it about to happen, and nothing came from it. I even got a message afterwards stating that they moved on from me after seeing it didn't work. But this is what I've done:
  • Step 1: Go to https://www.xbox.com/en-US/user/settings/privacy-and-safety
  • Step 2: Under the Others can: section, set the 3rd option to Friends or Blocked (whatever your preference) <---If you have this set to Anyone, they can still getcha!
  • Step 3: Open up GameBar with WinKey+G and log out of your account (NOTE: You don't have to uninstall GameBar)
  • Step 4: Ensure that the XBox App is not running in the background. You can end this task in the Task Manager
  • Step 5: In the in-game Sea of Thieves Settings, go into HUD and turn off Invite Notifications
  • Step 6: Scramble Gamertags in-game as well
Going this route will stop them 100%, no matter what platform you launch from. The ONLY downside to this is that you won't be able to hear in-game comms from anyone that isn't on your Friends List. (edited)
𝖙𝖔𝖝𝖎𝖊 𝖘𝖎𝖓𝖈𝖑𝖆𝖎𝖗𝖊
i had signed out of gamebar today and the invite spam worked still. friend had gamebar ended in task manager and it still happened to them
Spammals 9/06/2025 12:08 AM
Yea you really need to change the setting to not allow strangers to invite you. Thats the most important one. If you dont want to loose voice coms then all the other tips are just to try and mitigate as much as possible. (edited)
NateaCuiki 9/06/2025 12:31 AM
It's weird because I received a spam invite today, but it was almost at the end of my stream. I was already in the Sea of ​​Thieves main menu, and even though I disabled Game Bar widget notifications on Windows, they kept popping up. XD What I haven't noticed is my PC having trouble receiving these notifications. I don't know if it's related to some setting, but my PC hasn't slowed down or frozen, and I haven't noticed any performance drops. I play the game from the Microsoft Store version and never saw any in-game notifications; everything just sleeps in the background when I play the game in full screen

  • Step 1: Go to https://www.xbox.com/en-US/user/settings/privacy-and-safety

Sponge 13/06/2025 9:42 PM
The reason people are getting megs in hg is because of the new ones, or when a third party boat comes near a fight bringing a Meg with them, or if you invade someone with a Meg on them. The new megs are possibly the most frustrating thing to see in hg, because if you want to play hg you either have to play through one boat having a massive disadvantage (the Meg only bites/attacks one boat) or sail nearby an island and stay close to its border, or fight the Meg, which in my experience of a solo sloop game where we both shot at it (and I don’t think we are bad players who are missing) it took 15 minutes just to kill the Meg…

Captain Falcore 18/06/2025 9:19 AM
Yeah, Reapers Chests that are like semi permenant Bone Callers. And usual bug fixes, and I think the cross region stuff is coming in tomorrow? Not sure. But yeah, staggered updates only work when there is some beef to the update. Season 6 was a great example. Phantom Forts, followed by Adventure, followed by Legend of the Veil. The community is very bored, both new players and veterans. And the relevant feedback from drops is "These are worse than the ones we got before the revamp". Everythings wrong, just everything. This ain't the game I fell in love with.
Captain Falcore 18/06/2025 9:28 AM
I hate to sound like a doomsayer, but it's disheartening to watch this game deteriorate into a shadow of what it once was—whether it's the lack of meaningful content, underwhelming events, and diminished promotions/. The decline is hard to ignore. The community feels more disheartened than ever, and even the most optimistic players, the ones who would defend the developers through almost anything, are beginning to lose hope. Please try to understand, i'm not pointing blame or saying the work that's being done is bad. I love the fact that we get events and drops and always honoured to take part, i'm just saying it's simply not enough anymore to keep the game afloat. We need more gravity to updates, and less "filler". As a content creator and livestreamer for this game, it’s becoming increasingly difficult to stay positive and reassure the community that everything will be okay. When even some of the most publicly optimistic partners, including myself, are beginning to lose hope, it speaks volumes about the state of things. This isn’t about how long any of us have been playing. The concern isn't rooted in burnout or years spent with the game, it's about what’s changed in just the past couple of years. The shift has been noticeable. It’s simply not the same game we all fell in love with. The passion, the excitement, the energy, it feels like it’s slipping away from you guys. I genuinely hope you can reconnect with the spark that ignited this journey in the first place. Right now, it feels like you've lost your way. But I still believe there's a chance to turn things around. I truly hope you can rally as a team and bring us back to the game we once couldn’t get enough of. (edited)
4
2

The community is very bored, both new players and veterans. And the relevant feedback from drops is "These are worse than the ones we got before the revamp". Everythings wrong, just everything. OffenddCamel 24/06/2025 10:41 AM
Hey @Ophie do you fully own the rights to the covers? And were they under a minute or longer?
OffenddCamel 24/06/2025 11:09 AM
If you do you can counter it and they will unmute your video within 24h
OffenddCamel
Hey @Ophie do you fully own the rights to the covers? And were they under a minute or longer?
Ophie 24/06/2025 11:11 AM
No to both, unfortunately. They're full length covers of songs that already exist
Ophie
No to both, unfortunately. They're full length covers of songs that already exist
OffenddCamel 24/06/2025 11:23 AM
Yeah it’s a bit tricky. If it’s under 1 min, you’re usually safe, no strike or mute. But once it hits 3 mins, things get iffy with copy rights system. There’s a little trick though.. you can use the original TikTok sound for the song you’re covering while uploading, just lower its volume to zero and keep your own audio playing at 50. TikTok will still pick it up as using the original sound and that can actually help your video get pushed more outside your usual algorithm and might go viral. (edited)
LF MAVERICK 24/06/2025 11:54 AM

OffenddCamel 24/06/2025 12:06 PM

OffenddCamel
Yeah it’s a bit tricky. If it’s under 1 min, you’re usually safe, no strike or mute. But once it hits 3 mins, things get iffy with copy rights system. There’s a little trick though.. you can use the original TikTok sound for the song you’re covering while uploading, just lower its volume to zero and keep your own audio playing at 50. TikTok will still pick it up as using the original sound and that can actually help your video get pushed more outside your usual algorithm and might go viral. (edited)

PhuzzyBond 11/07/2025 12:55 AM
RP servers most of them have paid features and subscriptions, as for Minecraft, A lot of plugins are supported through Patreon and other subscriptions. If SoT was moddable then that would be the case, free Custom Seas, but ofcorse thats not possible, since its run by the official team, Subscription makes so much sense. In the one condition of continuous and priotized support to continue upgrading the tools . For example, to me, controlling red water and its location possibly can be one of the most important features. You can create pathways for races, bring back Arena, and so much more. Make everything in red water automatically disabled, like volcanoes, animations sounds and animals, to massively improve performance during the event you are running

11:26 PM
Captain Falcore 14/07/2025 6:32 PM
Honestly, it's been nearly 20 years since I started this content creation journey, and one thing I’ve always stood by is: “Don’t let the negativity get you down.” But lately especially with this game it’s been harder than ever to live by that. Whether you're streaming or making videos, the negativity is overwhelming and relentless. It’s not just aimed at big creators either; even people with just a handful of viewers are being hit with constant toxic comments. At times, I genuinely feel like I need counseling just to process it all. You’d think after a wholesome, community-driven event like that recent festival, people might start seeing the brighter side like we saw last year. But no it’s like none of that matters. No matter how positive I try to be, or how much effort I put into keeping my content uplifting, it all gets drowned out by a wave of hate and bitterness surrounding the game. And it’s not just me. I see it happening on everyone’s content. It’s exhausting, it’s demoralizing, and honestly… it’s just heartbreaking.
9
3
EiKaRRRamba 14/07/2025 6:43 PM
As someone who has been playing Sea of Thieves since its launch, I've developed a deep connection to the game and its original community. Unfortunately, over the years, I’ve noticed a drastic shift in both the game’s direction and the behavior of its player base. While Sea of Thieves has always been a PvEvP experience, there used to be a much stronger sense of cooperation and shared adventure. Nowadays, it often feels like most players are only interested in PvP, no matter the cost. This one sided focus fosters not only aggressive behavior but also a level of toxicity that wasn’t nearly as present in the earlier days. When Safer Seas was introduced, I had high hopes that it would bring back that feeling of community and relaxed PvE exploration. Sadly, those expectations were quickly dashed due to the many restrictions placed on this game mode. For long time players like myself, who enjoy leveling Emissaries and farming achievements, Safer Seas offers very little incentive. It feels more like an isolated side mode than a meaningful alternative to the main experience. I sincerely hope that Sea of Thieves will one day shift its focus back to the community and place greater emphasis on the PvE aspects of the game. But to be honest, I'm slowly starting to lose hope that this change will ever come.
1
Captain Falcore
Honestly, it's been nearly 20 years since I started this content creation journey, and one thing I’ve always stood by is: “Don’t let the negativity get you down.” But lately especially with this game it’s been harder than ever to live by that. Whether you're streaming or making videos, the negativity is overwhelming and relentless. It’s not just aimed at big creators either; even people with just a handful of viewers are being hit with constant toxic comments. At times, I genuinely feel like I need counseling just to process it all. You’d think after a wholesome, community-driven event like that recent festival, people might start seeing the brighter side like we saw last year. But no it’s like none of that matters. No matter how positive I try to be, or how much effort I put into keeping my content uplifting, it all gets drowned out by a wave of hate and bitterness surrounding the game. And it’s not just me. I see it happening on everyone’s content. It’s exhausting, it’s demoralizing, and honestly… it’s just heartbreaking.
2oldRudi 14/07/2025 7:41 PM
It’s getting harder and harder to tell the difference between people who are critical because they genuinely care about the game and want it to improve… and those who are just constantly negative for the sake of it. Some of them don’t even play the game anymore, they just hang around to complain and stir up negativity and enjoying that. I also feel like over the years, a part of the community has developed this sort of e-ego. It’s no longer just about being a pirate and having fun, for some, it’s about proving they’re better than others, and putting people down in the process etc.. The cheating issues definitely haven’t helped. They’ve brought a lot of frustration and mistrust, and that’s led to more toxic behavior overall. And when it gets like that, the people who just want to enjoy the game end up walking away :/ Some content creators are getting bullied just because they’re not good at TDM. Some partners are even being questioned about their partnership just because they don’t focus on PvP or Hourglass… it doesn’t really make sense. But despite all that, I still believe in this game and this community. There are so many passionate, kind, and creative people still sailing ❤️ Let's hope Custom Servers can change all of this and showcase creative, engaging projects around the game and not stay fixed on the negative/everyday side of the game
Captain Falcore
Honestly, it's been nearly 20 years since I started this content creation journey, and one thing I’ve always stood by is: “Don’t let the negativity get you down.” But lately especially with this game it’s been harder than ever to live by that. Whether you're streaming or making videos, the negativity is overwhelming and relentless. It’s not just aimed at big creators either; even people with just a handful of viewers are being hit with constant toxic comments. At times, I genuinely feel like I need counseling just to process it all. You’d think after a wholesome, community-driven event like that recent festival, people might start seeing the brighter side like we saw last year. But no it’s like none of that matters. No matter how positive I try to be, or how much effort I put into keeping my content uplifting, it all gets drowned out by a wave of hate and bitterness surrounding the game. And it’s not just me. I see it happening on everyone’s content. It’s exhausting, it’s demoralizing, and honestly… it’s just heartbreaking.
KroTukk 15/07/2025 12:52 PM
Thanks for sharing bud. I’ve struggled with this too. Like you, I’m one of the original batch of partners. I’ve been here since the very beginning. Brave vanguard is a title I cherish. But what you’re saying is a large part of why you lovely lot don’t hear from me much anymore. It’s unfortunately the case that every time I get back into the game, someone spoils the fun with toxicity. Whether it’s a comment, a VoIP experience, toxic behavior, or what have you, never fails. It’s havoc on my mental health. Honestly, it made me question if I even deserve to be among you all if all I ever do is play a couple times a week only to see a vile malignant word salad every time I cast off. Like, is something wrong with me? But it’s something in the community. We gotta work together to police this stuff. Not sure where the community started to turn, but it is definitely ill. I’ll echo what a few of ya said here, I truly hope that custom servers breathes some new positive light into the game and brings back that golden core i used to love so much. Especially when that will encourage more PVEVP shenanigans and hopefully bring more balance to the force. I’m definitely planning to unmoor my fleet and do some fun events again once that’s rolled out. The Copper Keg is gonna bust its doors open again. I plan on refocusing more energy into SoT in the near future for this reason, to get my sea legs back, because I believe the new and improved custom servers will be exactly what I need to start lighting beacons of positivity again. I’m looking forward to the new wave of pirates to join or rejoin the community for shenanigans around the seas with the new shenanigans yall have cookin. Myself being one of them.
3

MonkyJV 🇫🇷 16/07/2025 11:52 AM
Oh... My heart breaks as I read this, but I'll try to bring a little kindness, love and support for you @Captain Falcore. As I said to MotherOfChickens on Saturday, I'd love to have met you in person at SoTFest or even at the Village Hotel! Why would I do that? Quite simply because you've been my source of inspiration, my role model for embarking on this crazy adventure of creating content. I've been following you for years and I love all the videos you've produced, so I follow your streams from time to time and it's always a pleasure! I'm opening my heart, it's going to sound a bit like ass-kissing, but I don't care! I don't think enough is said about the positives, even beyond content creation. So I just wanted to say: thank you! Thank you for all the hard work you've put in, for the wonderful person that you are and that I've enjoyed following for so many years! That dream of meeting you is still alive, we've met twice in game and I want you to know that it's motivated me like never before to continue my adventure as a content creator. Somehow, thanks to you, I've had the motivation and strength to become a Sea of Thieves Partner and even a Twitch Partner, just with the simple words you told me! Not letting so much negativity get you down is easy to say, but very hard to do. On internet and not just on Sea of Thieves, people just get nasty and I think we all experience that. So I want to offer you my support, even if this message isn't much, I hope it does you some good. I'm sorry I didn't get to it sooner. Next year, I hope to see you at SoTFest26 and give you a real pirate hug and we'll talk about this story again, laughing and sharing a Guinness! Take care of yourself, take some time for yourself and above all take the time you need to come back in even better shape with your legendary humour! Stay strong! I love you, mate!
1
1
MonkyJV 🇫🇷
Oh... My heart breaks as I read this, but I'll try to bring a little kindness, love and support for you @Captain Falcore. As I said to MotherOfChickens on Saturday, I'd love to have met you in person at SoTFest or even at the Village Hotel! Why would I do that? Quite simply because you've been my source of inspiration, my role model for embarking on this crazy adventure of creating content. I've been following you for years and I love all the videos you've produced, so I follow your streams from time to time and it's always a pleasure! I'm opening my heart, it's going to sound a bit like ass-kissing, but I don't care! I don't think enough is said about the positives, even beyond content creation. So I just wanted to say: thank you! Thank you for all the hard work you've put in, for the wonderful person that you are and that I've enjoyed following for so many years! That dream of meeting you is still alive, we've met twice in game and I want you to know that it's motivated me like never before to continue my adventure as a content creator. Somehow, thanks to you, I've had the motivation and strength to become a Sea of Thieves Partner and even a Twitch Partner, just with the simple words you told me! Not letting so much negativity get you down is easy to say, but very hard to do. On internet and not just on Sea of Thieves, people just get nasty and I think we all experience that. So I want to offer you my support, even if this message isn't much, I hope it does you some good. I'm sorry I didn't get to it sooner. Next year, I hope to see you at SoTFest26 and give you a real pirate hug and we'll talk about this story again, laughing and sharing a Guinness! Take care of yourself, take some time for yourself and above all take the time you need to come back in even better shape with your legendary humour! Stay strong! I love you, mate!
Captain Falcore 16/07/2025 3:41 PM
3:42 PM
As for SoTFest, and other conventions, planning on being at each and every one next year, with the wife coming to abroad places!
3
Captain Falcore
Click to see attachment 🖼️
MonkyJV 🇫🇷 16/07/2025 7:44 PM
That's a very good news, can't wait to see you and your wife!
MonkyJV 🇫🇷 18/07/2025 8:47 AM
Just now in comments on my YouTube Channel. On an old video where I explain How to report toxic and cheater player : https://youtu.be/vd5dL-x5DtU "It's pretty, it's immersive, but some things make it unpleasant. Like not being able to kick a toxic person off your own boat. And some players who make the game unplayable for many other reasons, the problem is that you can't play alone, which is a shame, it's still a multiplayer game. Desperately empty servers. When you do find a few players, it's direct aggression, nothing constructive. A game with some adventure potential that ends up being a shooter only. This game is going to lose a lot of players because of that... I don't play it any more." This testimony from an exhausted player is true. What's more, it matches what Captain Falcore told us above. But I insist, you mustn't stop doing what you love, playing games you enjoy because of the bad behaviour of certain people. I replied to my subscriber that there are communities of like-minded pirates who share the same values as him, and that it's easy to join them! On the other hand, he's right that you can't exclude a pirate from your own boat. I think we need this option, because we've all had a crew member who does or says the wrong thing, whether in Opencrew or in gameviewers.
MonkyJV

Captain Falcore 24/07/2025 8:57 PM
They executed it perfectly, what I like to call the “Red Hot Chili Peppers” approach. It started with a solid bassline: the drops. That laid the foundation and got players hyped about the new game and cosmetics. Just as everyone was settling into the groove, bam!, in came the hi-hats and vocals: “Support a Streamer,” complete with added incentives. Now, they’ve smartly extended that initial momentum with a continuation of the themed drops, ones that tie directly into the current build’s lore. A nice touch of rhythm and storytelling. Food for thought, Rare.

Sonicbob (Drew) 5/09/2025 1:00 PM
@Gothicsnowangel @RocketManKian - We're continuing to work in this space to try and address this, we've found the root of the issue but it's pretty deep in the character loading system. It's very easy to get your account hacked on any platform. You receive a link, you click on it, it takes you to what looks like a Microsoft sign in page, and then you try to sign in while not thinking about it. We always have to be careful about clicking any links from any source
Captain Falcore 9/09/2025 5:21 PM
You learn as you go on, to never click any links, even from friends. I know ironically i'm now telling you to click on a link, but every single link that isn't from a legitimate source, even if its from a friend (they could be comprimsed) I run it through this first https://www.f-secure.com/gb-en/link-checker

Spammals 12/09/2025 12:24 PM
This is a hard topic to talk about without sounding like a twat. Because on one hand I’d love them to be everyone drops. But on the other hand I’d really like partner drops to continue. I’m happy larger streamers don’t feel any need for them anymore, but they still make a meaningful impact for smaller partners. Who have worked hard to put the time in, grow and get partner. Especially now more than ever trying to earn a living. 2024 saw 9 everyone drops to 6 Partner drops. This was raised as a topic in a recent call. So it’s not like everyone drops is a minority thing, until recently it was bigger. Other games such as RUST or 7 Days To Die all offer partner only drops. This isn’t unique to SoT. I’m also not sure about the loud and clear part of the announcement, I didn’t get a survey or see a poll, but it’s for sure made it very awkward for people to step forward and share their feelings on it because it makes you sound anti-community which is unfair and untrue. The Partner program is in a terrible state, every time I look in here I have a negative experience. If you voice an opinion you get attacked and an overall feeling of uselessness. Jumping the gun on this one has only made things worse.
4
NessieDoes 12/09/2025 12:26 PM
had a longer thought and I think everyone drops is a postive thing for the community, but I also agree with everything spammals has just said. gl team with the decision ❤️ (edited)
Spammals
This is a hard topic to talk about without sounding like a twat. Because on one hand I’d love them to be everyone drops. But on the other hand I’d really like partner drops to continue. I’m happy larger streamers don’t feel any need for them anymore, but they still make a meaningful impact for smaller partners. Who have worked hard to put the time in, grow and get partner. Especially now more than ever trying to earn a living. 2024 saw 9 everyone drops to 6 Partner drops. This was raised as a topic in a recent call. So it’s not like everyone drops is a minority thing, until recently it was bigger. Other games such as RUST or 7 Days To Die all offer partner only drops. This isn’t unique to SoT. I’m also not sure about the loud and clear part of the announcement, I didn’t get a survey or see a poll, but it’s for sure made it very awkward for people to step forward and share their feelings on it because it makes you sound anti-community which is unfair and untrue. The Partner program is in a terrible state, every time I look in here I have a negative experience. If you voice an opinion you get attacked and an overall feeling of uselessness. Jumping the gun on this one has only made things worse.
eBacon1_ 12/09/2025 12:27 PM
completely agree here, i love that there is a chance for everyone drops but as someone who is a very small partner and not as well known partner drops really help me out
jjgaymer☠ 12/09/2025 12:49 PM
So, totally hear you all here and listening to the feedback. I will tell you where my head is at currently. I think there is a balance to be struck here and happy to hear ideas around this. I know everyone will have different opinions and we can't please everybody. There is an element of "rising tide lifts all boats", and we do see an uplift in the category as a whole when everyone drops are on. However, I do want to balance this with giving making the Partners an element of exclusivity . I am happy to hear ideas on how we could do both moving forward. The next set of drops will be "Everyone Drops" but we have an opportunity to rearrange how we distribute them for October and moving forward. I am thinking along the lines of Partners over the weekend (Fri - Mon) then Everyone else for the rest of the week (Mon - Fri) Now atm this would be just splitting and moving drops we already have as the cosmetics for next year are just starting to be put through production now. Example could be: Partner Drops - Friday to Monday Gold/Voyage/Legacy Cosmetic/New Cosmetic Everyone Drops - Monday to Friday Renown/Legacy Cosmetic/Emporium Cosmetic Just an example but happy to hear others. For context we can only run one campaign on Twitch at a time with their new setup so that limits things
6
eBacon1_ 12/09/2025 12:57 PM
i would be happy with partner drops starting then opening it up to everyone
Esteri 12/09/2025 1:02 PM
I like the idea of Fri-Mon partner drops and rest of the week everyone. I think it feels balanced and keeps the perk of having exclusive drops as a partner alive while also allowing other streamers to have a chance to grow and if im honest make some extra $ BUT also still encourage people to keep working hard towards SoT partner (assuming thats their goal) cause being able to have access to drops was certainly one of mine!

I don’t mention this to be all “it’s unfair” not at all, but rather to question why people running the partner program havent briefed in new items for us to replace the widely disliked Serp Sails. BehavingBeardly 12/09/2025 6:38 PM
Just going back to Drops and put my bit in. I dropped it in Drops Feedback chat earlier this week. "Partner drops were always an incentive for people to push for partner, put out better content for SoT, and create regular 'positive' content for SoT in order to achieve that goal. Taking the Partner Drops away does de value that 'goal' (if I was to put my business head on) ." For me.... I don't mind, but it's easy for me to say that as someone that been a partner for 5 years. I think theres some very valid concerns here. Im not sure the current system had an issue? But rather the 'consistency' of scheduling/system is far more important... cos it's getting confusing for me let alone viewers So TLDR. I think its in the interest of the eco system in the long run to have exclusivity to the PP to keep it appealing and have people want to strive towards it. And it's important that all Drops activations follow the same 'routine/system' to avoid confusion.
2
1
Captain Falcore 12/09/2025 9:32 PM
The funny thing is, whenever we talk about adding more perks for partners, the main counterpoint is always: “But you already get direct access to the devs.” And honestly, that’s fair, I agree with it. I can reach out to Epic Mike to ask about lore or other details and know I’ll get a response. From the outside, though, I can understand why the incentive might look a little underwhelming, especially with fewer Partner-Exclusive Drops. But really, shouldn’t the main incentive be about being closer to the development process itself? If someone’s only in it for a few exclusive cosmetics to boost views, then they’re in it for the wrong reason. Just to be clear, this isn’t meant as an argument, more of a thought I wanted to put out there.
9:35 PM
This is why I keep raising the concern that, as partners, we don’t really feel connected to the development process anymore. Season 18 details came out through an Insiders post, and there wasn’t even a Partner Call. That’s what I’m here for, I want to feel like I’m contributing, giving feedback, and helping shape the future of the game. I’m not here just to chase clout or cosmetics. Again! This is a general statement, not an argument.
9:39 PM
To be real, I’ll absolutely use those cosmetics for clout and views, we all do, no matter the gamem it's our job.. But at the end of the day, the real reason I want to be partnered with any game is to have my voice heard, to give input, and to help shape the update cadence alongside other like-minded people. That’s the real sauce I’m chasing! That should always be the driving force. (edited)

HitboTC 15/09/2025 1:24 PM
I want to be honest — the Partner Program feels lost. What was once prestigious now feels overlooked, and sometimes it even seems like there are those who’d rather the program didn’t exist at all. It doesn’t sit right with me that non-partners are voicing opinions in partner-only discussions. (I agree with @Spar) And on top of that, it doesn’t make sense that partners don’t get the latest giveaways. We’ve worked incredibly hard to be here, and while I’m glad other teams get opportunities, giving partners the leftovers just reinforces the feeling that we’re overlooked. I also think we should remove the “Partner Legend” distinction and bring everyone back to simply being “Partners.” At the end of the day, we’re all in this together. I say this because I care. It’s sad to see the state of things right now, and I truly hope we can bring back the pride and purpose the Partner Program once had.

NateaCuiki 15/09/2025 3:36 PM
Many of the problems we're experiencing, including the discussions here, would be solved if Rare would put all its programs and systems in order. The game itself has been testing formats for years, trying to find the best way to deliver content and work on development. It feels like we're going through endless trial and error, year after year, and for a game that's been on the market for seven years, this is disconcerting, to say the least, because it should already be at a stage of knowing which format to maintain stably. This same chaos has been seen in the management of its partner and creator programs. Since I've been here, I've seen many changes: in drops, new ranks, variety streamers, partner legends, and now the stream team. Honestly, in the end, all of this has created a hodgepodge that has blurred the order of everything. We have Partner Legends, which doesn't offer any perk upgrades in the program, and that's useless; it only creates frustration and arguments among all the partners. Currently, it seems Rare wants to create star creators for its game, rather than focusing on discovering potential creators. Forcing star creators isn't the same as discovering them because they are. Most of the partners here were discovered, not created. The game's poor health and its content roadmap make it difficult for new creators interested in showcasing the game. Fixing that should be the priority. A fun game creates an engaged community, and from an engaged community, creators willing to make videos and streams of your game are born. Currently, the game's health is poor, and that's causing fewer and fewer creators and streamers instead of new ones. Partners have even stopped playing or making videos because they aren't motivated by the game's current state. I imagine Rare is struggling to find new creators for the partner program because few creators are emerging in their game's category. The problem is that as long as the game's health is poor, there will only be a loss of audiences and, therefore, fewer quality creators who can actually apply to the partner program.
3:37 PM
This whole situation, coupled with the disarray in the programs, is the current situation we have. Taking away more benefits from Partners to give them to a group of creators so they can one day join the Partner Program, doesn't make logical sense if you think about it. I think things need to be tidied up a bit and there needs to be an appropriate perk scale. If the final rank is to reach Partner status, Partners are the ones who should have the most special perks. I understand that Rare wants to discover new creative talent, but I think they're putting obstacles in their own way. In my opinion, the stream team isn't necessary; there's already the usual Creators Team for that. Drops for everyone at the beginning of a new season and during the community weekend should be enough to identify creators who have the potential to become future Partners. And if there aren't enough viewers, it's perhaps because the game isn't engaging enough to motivate new creators to make videos or stream it. I'll also point out that the decline in Drop quality over the past few years has also led to fewer and fewer audiences seeking to collect these cosmetics, so this tool also needs to be better utilized. Logically, Everyone Drops should be old Drops that have already been released, but that are interesting. And Partner Drops should be completely new cosmetics. The need isn't for quantity, but for quality. Two or three really nice new cosmetics would certainly attract attention. They would also end up being used for Everyone Drops in the future, and as such, new creators could be discovered who would also boost audiences with them. (I often see new monthly cosmetics being sold for Gold in the game's Outposts, which could have been potential Partner Drops... just saying. Even the cosmetics we'll be able to buy for gold in this month's update are nicer than what we'll receive as rewards for participating in the Heist event) Finally, I must say that I say all this out of the love I have for this game and I don't mean it in a disrespectful way. I often think about the adversities Rare has endured over the years, including layoffs from Microsoft, and so many things we'll never know about, so I have complete empathy for that. But right now the situation is complicated, and it even saddens me to see fights between the people on this Discord, fights that wouldn't exist if certain things were put in order. (edited)
8

I understand that Rare wants to discover new creative talent, but I think they're putting obstacles in their own way. In my opinion, the stream team isn't necessary; there's already the usual Creators Team for that. Drops for everyone at the beginning of a new season and during the community weekend should be enough to identify creators who have the potential to become future Partners. And if there aren't enough viewers, it's perhaps because the game isn't engaging enough to motivate new creators to make videos or stream it. I'll also point out that the decline in Drop quality over the past few years has also led to fewer and fewer audiences seeking to collect these cosmetics, so this tool also needs to be better utilized. Logically, Everyone Drops should be old Drops that have already been released, but that are interesting. And Partner Drops should be completely new cosmetics. The need isn't for quantity, but for quality. Two or three really nice new cosmetics would certainly attract attention. They would also end up being used for Everyone Drops in the future, and as such, new creators could be discovered who would also boost audiences with them. (I often see new monthly cosmetics being sold for Gold in the game's Outposts, which could have been potential Partner Drops... just saying. Even the cosmetics we'll be able to buy for gold in this month's update are nicer than what we'll receive as rewards for participating in the Heist event) Finally, I must say that I say all this out of the love I have for this game and I don't mean it in a disrespectful way. I often think about the adversities Rare has endured over the years, including layoffs from Microsoft, and so many things we'll never know about, so I have complete empathy for that. But right now the situation is complicated, and it even saddens me to see fights between the people on this Discord, fights that wouldn't exist if certain things were put in order. (edited)
Spar 15/09/2025 3:44 PM
nailed it i wish i was as good as you to explaining things. I just remember when I joined years ago and how the partner program was and definitely more people talked but just more campaigns for newer creators. Just seemed more of a focus for partners but I also know things are different now
Capt Thorgal 15/09/2025 6:13 PM
Hi, personally I don't agree, for the drops go to everyone. 1. For example, since then I've come across players/streamers with 2 or 3 viewers who stream snipe, glitch and use the N Word, very poor viability. We reported it and 2 days later we met her again with a new deposit. 2. We've worked hard to get here for almost 5 years, we play fair, we encourage viewers to follow us and play without using the wrong things. 3. It gives the impression that the more than 11,500 hours of play doing good work is worthless. Sorry, I don't speak English well.

RocketManKian 16/09/2025 6:56 AM

3
PhuzzyBond 16/09/2025 1:53 PM
Generally everything in the world is designed in ladders and perks, because that's how we are. You work hard, you get a promotion, you get extra benefits. You slack off, you get a long hard stare, you lose benefits. Tier Partnered System. Example: here are partners that haven't touched the game in 6 months (unless there's a real life reason), why should they get same benefits as active ones? On the other hand, some of the younger partners work harder than several partner legends combined, and they are very active in providing feedback to the game with full dedication, why are they getting the same benefits of the ones that haven't touched the game in 6 months? I had the privilege to be one of the oldest partners here, there were about 15 partners when i joined, I've seen this program go through all sorts and changes. A tiered / non-bias system is fair for all, With an entry point on Lurkit (Creator Crew), then level up to Stream Team, then after that higher, and so on, and from there you go under the CM radar (hours watched / streams .. etc) Then perks will be done based on these stats. When the streamer can SEE a path, the streamer can and will plan ahead, but right now, its mostly blind hope, they dont know if they will get a reply, approved, ignored, and so on. (edited)

@Sonicbob (Drew) - i've just had a near 45 minute discussion with a CMH user. They've just released a patch to the software which is very bad (release was around midnight UK Time). Ive got screenshots from their pov of the following (i dont know if im good to send that in here) *Their view of the encounter (including in the top right a variety of things they can do, including water walk) *A screenshot of the server notifying when they match a streamer *A screenshot of the 'tags' - this is vulger and includes slurs *A video (not sure if safe to click, i have not watched) The cheater confirmed the following: *They can only teleport to the bed *They can steal any loot item they can hold in their hand, including the new kegs - which can then come with them to any bed (this means they can do the equiv of keg dropping) *They can hop servers to any server via specific requirements (ie: they can state "i want a skeleton fort" or "i want a streamer") *They can only view names within 2000m, but once they see the name itll mark via specific logs *When they match a 'namer' itll ping their discord with a twitch link, so everyone can watch *They can see the damage of ships, they can see water lvl and holes *They have a 'rear view mirror' that can see the 3rd person of 1st person of any pirate on the server *They can see if anything has been spawned with 2000m, ie: skeleton galleon *Magic bucket is CURRENTLY patched *Machine gun is still in the game *Wallbang only works through one layer *If they teleport to a bed whilst harpooning a player it will teleport them to said bed, though outside of the ship hull *They can also see through hidden names, and see titles (the cheater tp'd to me through this, and say noble of the waters) (edited)

NateaCuiki 1/11/2025 12:49 AM
It's so funny for me when people say: "Don't release new seasons, we don't care if there's no new season if you spend the next four months fixing bugs." But then, when a one-month delay is announced for new content, and Drew mentions they'll be implementing improvements and fixes, people immediately react: "Another delay, dead game, I've run out of content to complete, blah blah."
1
NateaCuiki
It's so funny for me when people say: "Don't release new seasons, we don't care if there's no new season if you spend the next four months fixing bugs." But then, when a one-month delay is announced for new content, and Drew mentions they'll be implementing improvements and fixes, people immediately react: "Another delay, dead game, I've run out of content to complete, blah blah."
Captain Falcore 1/11/2025 12:58 AM
People know what they want, and then they get what they want, and they don't like it. We're all guility of it though! Example right here, I wanted the Lego Monorail set from 1987, and I managed to get one on auction from Ebay, I thought this would be a life changing event. Turns out the Lego Monorail set from 1987 was just really BAD but I had lower standards back then Hahaha, no, but I agree. I think the main issue, and this isn’t meant as shade, just an honest conversation between us, is that we keep hearing about all these amazing things that are supposedly coming, new focuses, better ways to tackle performance issues, toxicity, hacking, content cadence, and updates that will make everything better, It’s always “just around the corner,” but it never actually happens. At this point, the game is getting maybe 20% of the content it did just three years ago, with no major performance improvements aside from a brief spike at the start of Season 15. Since then, things have only gone downhill. Morale is really low right now, and it’s starting to show. (edited)
1:01 AM
If less content meant better perfromance, i'd be fine with it. But gotta be real here, its just less content and worse performance.
NateaCuiki 1/11/2025 1:04 AM
I agree with what you're saying. I've also constantly felt that way, that everything will get better just around the corner, that the next season is going to be the one, and then disappointing things happen Unfortunately, I suspect that all the Xbox studios are struggling to stay afloat right now, and Rare is trying to find a way to keep delivering something good with the limited resources they seem to have had for some time now. So I try to be compassionate in that regard.
Captain Falcore 1/11/2025 1:05 AM
Yeah like I said, no shade being thrown at the devs at all. But something is up! Someone is doing bad things for the game, pulling the wrong strings.

LF MAVERICK 2/11/2025 8:40 PM
@Admiral Good evening everyone, Is it possible to reduce the explosion power or range of the smuggler's barrel? I play a lot, and in my sessions, we only sink ships from large skeleton ship barrels with the long range (PvE). We also encounter this problem in PvP adventures. There are WAY too many of these barrels on the islands. Their explosion power is far too great. I've discussed this with my community, and many players are getting frustrated and have stopped playing the game because of this barrel. I think this is an important topic for discussion. I understand that the development team is doing its best for the health of the game, but I think something needs to be done. Too many people are fed up. Best wishes Maverick (edited)
LF MAVERICK
@Admiral Good evening everyone, Is it possible to reduce the explosion power or range of the smuggler's barrel? I play a lot, and in my sessions, we only sink ships from large skeleton ship barrels with the long range (PvE). We also encounter this problem in PvP adventures. There are WAY too many of these barrels on the islands. Their explosion power is far too great. I've discussed this with my community, and many players are getting frustrated and have stopped playing the game because of this barrel. I think this is an important topic for discussion. NateaCuiki 6/11/2025 9:28 PM
Since Twitch started cracking down on viewbots this summer, the platform has been very unstable for all streamers. During the September Drops, I had some really weird moments on my streams, where viewership would suddenly jump by 50 or 100, then drop again, and then jump back up. I even thought someone was using bots on my streams, but then I started reading comments from many creators of different content saying they were experiencing similar issues, so I stopped worrying. It's not something I could fix anyway. I've also seen streamers on social media saying they're having problems with viewer counts when using the Broadcast Enhanced option. I think Twitch is changing so many things on its platform lately, adding features like streamer together, shared chat, shared counters, and so on, that it's causing all these anomalies we're seeing these days.
NateaCuiki
Since Twitch started cracking down on viewbots this summer, the platform has been very unstable for all streamers. During the September Drops, I had some really weird moments on my streams, where viewership would suddenly jump by 50 or 100, then drop again, and then jump back up. I even thought someone was using bots on my streams, but then I started reading comments from many creators of different content saying they were experiencing similar issues, so I stopped worrying. It's not something I could fix anyway. I've also seen streamers on social media saying they're having problems with viewer counts when using the Broadcast Enhanced option. I think Twitch is changing so many things on its platform lately, adding features like streamer together, shared chat, shared counters, and so on, that it's causing all these anomalies we're seeing these days.

While the use of Third-Party Tools is against the Sea of Thieves Code of Conduct, the use of Software, Monitor features or Physical solutions to overlay a Crosshair on the screen will not be a bannable offence if used for Sea of Thieves. https://support.seaofthieves.com/articles/24643308439314-Support-Enforcement-Policy-Updates

JemmaFoxsong 14/11/2025 11:12 PM

1
Gothicsnowangel
Heres something ive been wanting to ask for a long time: Anyone know whats the deal behind those random AFK ships that are just left on outposts for no reason with no players anywhere nearby?
LDSylvr 15/11/2025 4:31 AM
When a player doesn’t exit the game correctly, whether it’s an internet loss, HazelnutBeard, turning off the power button to their console, or just a simple Alt-F4… the server will “reserve” their spot for 10-15min in case they come back. So anytime you see a ship just sitting there with no player in sight, whether it’s a fresh spawn or a ship that just finished turning in all their loot, it’s simply because they left the game incorrectly.
4:31 AM
================== Congrats @eBacon1_

Puff 8/12/2025 12:06 AM
Hi friends and colleagues! I have to say that I'm quite sad and also disturbed by the drama that's been taking place on socials lately. I want to remind you that you are being watched by your community members and your colleagues communities as well. You're also being role models to people. I've followed the drama from the side line as I simply don't enjoy partaking in it. But what I can gather is that everyone in it that is in one way or another associated with Sea of Thieves has said things that shouldn't be said. For those things you should suck it up and for fuck (yes) sake apologise each other for. Voicing opinions to the extent it's being brought into personal targeting is not okay. We are adults and I know you guys know this. It's also not necessary to bring up the conversations live when everything in the first place could've happen in DMs. Yes, the whole conversation started in public and got responded to there. Doesn't mean that the extended and more parties needed to continue it. I want to add that being opinionated is not wrong, but it should be done gracefully. Questioning others way of streaming/gaming is not okay. Bringing up family is not okay. Misgendering and calling names is not okay. A lot was not okay. We need to accept our differences and opinions. Agree to disagree. We co-exist here and you should damn it behave. It's Christmas month, so Happy damn Christmas. I needed to get this off my chest because I HATE to see this.
4
Puff
Hi friends and colleagues! I have to say that I'm quite sad and also disturbed by the drama that's been taking place on socials lately. I want to remind you that you are being watched by your community members and your colleagues communities as well. You're also being role models to people. I've followed the drama from the side line as I simply don't enjoy partaking in it. But what I can gather is that everyone in it that is in one way or another associated with Sea of Thieves has said things that shouldn't be said. For those things you should suck it up and for fuck (yes) sake apologise each other for. Voicing opinions to the extent it's being brought into personal targeting is not okay. We are adults and I know you guys know this. It's also not necessary to bring up the conversations live when everything in the first place could've happen in DMs. Yes, the whole conversation started in public and got responded to there. Doesn't mean that the extended and more parties needed to continue it. I want to add that being opinionated is not wrong, but it should be done gracefully. Questioning others way of streaming/gaming is not okay. Bringing up family is not okay. Misgendering and calling names is not okay. A lot was not okay. We need to accept our differences and opinions. Agree to disagree. We co-exist here and you should damn it behave. It's Christmas month, so Happy damn Christmas. I needed to get this off my chest because I HATE to see this.
Flux 8/12/2025 12:36 AM
Couldnt agree more with this. I've also been seeing some stuff from the sidelines and what I saw wasn't a good look. The game is undeniably already in a super rough spot at the moment and the last thing SOT needs is it's partners and creators acting like little kids (sorry but true) over a disagreement
𝖙𝖔𝖝𝖎𝖊 𝖘𝖎𝖓𝖈𝖑𝖆𝖎𝖗𝖊 8/12/2025 1:09 AM
@LDSylvr my pronouns are she her not he him. you had my twitter profile up and could see it written. ur chat even told u im trans. changed man though. you also allowed your chat to say some awful things about me, including a consistency to misgender me there too, which btw is against ur OWN chat rules (edited)
1
Puff
Hi friends and colleagues! I have to say that I'm quite sad and also disturbed by the drama that's been taking place on socials lately. I want to remind you that you are being watched by your community members and your colleagues communities as well. You're also being role models to people. I've followed the drama from the side line as I simply don't enjoy partaking in it. But what I can gather is that everyone in it that is in one way or another associated with Sea of Thieves has said things that shouldn't be said. For those things you should suck it up and for fuck (yes) sake apologise each other for. Voicing opinions to the extent it's being brought into personal targeting is not okay. We are adults and I know you guys know this. It's also not necessary to bring up the conversations live when everything in the first place could've happen in DMs. Yes, the whole conversation started in public and got responded to there. Doesn't mean that the extended and more parties needed to continue it. I want to add that being opinionated is not wrong, but it should be done gracefully. Questioning others way of streaming/gaming is not okay. Bringing up family is not okay. Misgendering and calling names is not okay. A lot was not okay. We need to accept our differences and opinions. Agree to disagree. We co-exist here and you should damn it behave. It's Christmas month, so Happy damn Christmas. I needed to get this off my chest because I HATE to see this.
SlimeyPigeon 8/12/2025 9:02 AM
"Questioning others way of streaming/gaming is not okay". I want to clarify this was stated as my suspicion is that support a streamer became support a partner because they want to entice people back who are playing Arc currently (from a business standpoint, this makes sense, from a goodwill standpoint, this is beyond silly). It comes a place of frustration and a feeling of neglect from Rare. I at no point disagree with variety, and have clarified this in later points. (I do it myself) My intention was to never shame other creators, it was to point out why this is likely occurring. This is on me for poorly wording, but I hope later clarification has ironed this out so people know my intent of this, I encourage anyone to do whatever they see fit in terms of gaming on twitch. I was then retweeted and shamed by a community because it got to someone. Theyve then accused me of doing things then know weren't my intention and have clarified, rallied a community far larger than anything I could muster and shattered my mental health the entire weekend (I was literally in tears at multiple points). It felt like David vs Goliath. I'd also like to point out I offered to take it DMs, because I saw it getting out of hand, but was refused by said partner, so the spitting continued. I could've 100% acted way better over this situation, and trust me, I whole-heartedly regret not just shutting up. I went on the aggressive to defend myself, and shouldve just left it. This needs to stop. Having been on the receiving end and giving end, it isn't worth it. It broke me in a time where we need to work together to try and get meaningful change. We all agree that these partner only 3 gifted drops are stupid - lets stick together on this. (edited)
1
SlimeyPigeon 8/12/2025 9:12 AM
https://x.com/Slimey_Pigeon/status/1997694547661602892
Slimey Pigeon (@Slimey_Pigeon)
I think we've all had enough drama now, lets move on. (I'm tired and stressed) Despite our differences, we do agree on one thing, these drops should be for all. A rising tide helps all boats. Make it happen @SeaOfThieves (enjoy my peak photoshop and cosplay). #SeaOfThieves

Elmuchacho 8/12/2025 9:36 AM
In my opinion, it's good that "Support a Streamer" is now "Support a Partner." This year, partners didn't get much new stuff. There were hardly any partner drops; almost all drops were for every streamer. And as giveaways, we got the same old stuff from the streamer crew. So I think it's justified that it's only for partners.
Elmuchacho
In my opinion, it's good that "Support a Streamer" is now "Support a Partner." This year, partners didn't get much new stuff. There were hardly any partner drops; almost all drops were for every streamer. And as giveaways, we got the same old stuff from the streamer crew. So I think it's justified that it's only for partners.
SlimeyPigeon 8/12/2025 9:45 AM
Drops should be for everyone. This includes partner, stream team etc. Partners will still pull in more numbers / bigger numbers with drops for everyone, this just allows everyone to benefit / grow. It also doesn't sit right that this is a 'paid for' drop, given only to partners. It feels, icky. If it was a 'not paid for' drop then i'd still be a bit meh about it, but it being 'paid for' just doesn't sit right with me. Feels gross. These drops are also for 3 weeks. It'll heal the category temporarily for 3 weeks, but after such time itll likely hurt more as some creators who may feel alienated leave, as well as partners who, quite rightly and what they feel is best for them / their community, sail for fresher waters. The category is hurting, big time, now is not the time to alienate those who aren't partner who stream.
