NessieDoes 8/12/2025 11:30 AM
In case any rare peeps are reading over this I think that... New giveaway items first to stream team the same year that drops became everyone drops made partner "less valuable". That cant really be argued. If someone with an established audience was partner and swapped to stream team, they would have a leg up on others with these new items to draw clicks. I think it is very opinionated but I think it really should have been one or the other, not both at the same time. I saw someone before say drops should always be everyone drops. While I was for all that change I do understand rare doing partner only drops for such a long period, being able to have partners represent the game on update days is pretty smart. I remember on one update day I found a silly exploit/bug and was getting carried away with it, got a dm asking me to stop, and it stops. Rare having that control to make sure "approved" content is the stuff being displayed is just a good business decision, but everyone drops is a good community decision so I have no issue with it. https://discord.com/channels/550315686636027908/969521803405955122/1446926020153184392 I would like this answered if possible as having some insight into the decision making would be nice, as from both a community and business viewpoint this change to support a streamer is def stirring the pot.

SlimeyPigeon 8/12/2025 12:16 PM
Hitbo - please stop tagging me, or interacting with me. You caused me a great deal of stress this weekend, and youre unable to understand as a creator of your size targetting me, who is far smaller, is going to cause a great deal of damage, which oddly upsets me a lot more at the moment. You could've just replied. You instead retweeted it, garnering your fanbase who then went onto insult my work, insult me personally and in general make me feel terrible. I spent the Sunday morning on the couch, in and out of mental relapse for my diagnosed depression and anxiety. I said my peace in the tweet, poorly worded yes (I am very dyslexic, so I am quick to admit if something is poorly worded), but worthy of you leading an attack with your community (which is what you did, even if you dont view it this way), nope. You arent to know about my diagnosed mental problems, but as a creator of your size you need to be aware pointing a finger at anyone in any form of negative light can lead an attack, which this did, and there is a human behind that screen. Genuinely, I have nothing more to say to you. I've been trying to move on, but you rear your head whenever I am about to. Please. Stop. (edited)

Puff 8/12/2025 12:25 PM
Anyways. My message was originated to remind you that is was being watched by the public eye and your peers in this programme. I wasn't intending bloss up more stress or anger from anyone here and I hope you guys know that. In the end I was hoping for a agree to disagree with our differences. To me it's important to at least try to see what the other one has to say. Not always agree, but understand where they are coming from and move on. We are people with emotions and a backpack with life stuff we don't know about. We need to be kind to each other and support one another where we can. I would've preferred to see apologies made in the places they are needed. Now ima stop rambling. That's my TED talk. And yes, I'm grinding those stupid ass 5 million atm. I have 1.9mill to go.

LDSylvr 8/12/2025 3:44 PM
@𝖙𝖔𝖝𝖎𝖊 𝖘𝖎𝖓𝖈𝖑𝖆𝖎𝖗𝖊 here's the thing... I understand that being trans in a world that is still struggling to accept it must be one of, if not, the most difficult things to have to go through in life. I get it. Yesterday when I said "he", it was an honest mistake, at first. My first speech about the whole thing was just speaking my mind on the matter and I misgendered you, unintentionally because I don't know you and have had zero interactions with you before. That was my mistake and I apologize for that. I'm old and I try to keep up with as much as I can with what's going on, but some things do tend to get past me. Later in the stream, I'm looking at Twitter and I see the tweets about misgendering you, which still had totally slipped past me, and I see your post about telling me that, "you're not entitled to forgiveness", and I felt that was very disrespectful. So my response was, "you're not entitled to respect". My whole thing is this Toxie, I COMPLETELY understand how being misgendered can feel. I understand so much because it's the same feeling as being mislabeled. When I brought up bringing Hitbo's wife into the matter was wrong and she was owed an apology, you immediately attacked me and mislabeled me by stating, "there's literally clips of you saying the f slur". Why would you do that? Why would you label me by something from my past that I have moved on from? The same goes with me misgendering you. That's something from your past that you have moved on from and I wrong having done so. And again, I apologize for that. Bottom line Toxie, I'm willing to move on from this. I don't expect your forgiveness from my past, but just know that it's something I've learned from and haven't repeated. That word was something that was used in every day vocabulary from where I came from. I'm not saying that as an excuse either, but rather saying that as an accomplishment, for me, to not have repeated that mistake. To remove something so common and never repeat it again. That's a HUGE accomplishment for me that I'm very proud of... but to have thrown back in my face and mislabeling me is what upsets me, so I KNOW you KNOW exactly where I'm coming from. I'll be at SoTFest2026. I'm willing to sit down, talk, have a beer and chill out together if you are because at the end of the day, we're all EQUAL human beings, regardless of beliefs, stances and views. Nothing in this world puts someone else's value as a person higher than the person standing next to them. I hope we can come to terms on this and move past it, ma'am. You have a good day!

NateaCuiki 8/12/2025 6:49 PM
I won't lie, I miss how things were in 2021 when I joined the program, but that's because everything was simpler and clearer. If you became a Partner, you got Drops, exclusive Giveaways, and you could act as an intermediary between your community and the Devs by being on this Discord server, and that was it. And if you weren't a Partner, you had a clear goal to try and achieve. Right now, most creators in my language have stopped caring about ever becoming Partners. What's the point? They already get Drops every month, just like Partners before to, and interest in the Giveaways started to decrease the day we began giving away Serpent's Lie Sails. It took far too long to change the plan and allow us to give away new interesting things. You'd be surprised by how little interest Spanish-speakers people have in giveaways right now. I can't even get them to care much when I give away the Gilded Phoenix Crest These days, the only thing I can be proud of is that the people who come to watch me live do so because they like me, because giveaways certainly don't generate much extra boost. Lately, I've read a lot about opinions on supporting smaller creators. Most of the time, when this is said, it's based on a single scenario where Sea of ​​Thieves partners are all big creators with thousands of viewers every day. The reality is that there are Sea of ​​Thieves partners, especially non-English language ones, who are just as "small" as non-partner English streamers. My fellow Spanish partners have between 20-35 viewers on a typical day, and it's not Sea of ​​Thieves' fault. The Spanish audience has been decrease across the entire Twitch platform for the last two years, and it continues to decrease. That's why every time I read people talking about SoT partners here as if we were all rich or super famous, it makes me sad. I've read several people mention that last year's Support a Streamer campaign was wonderful and helped small streamers. I'm very glad that was the case for some, but do you want to know what happened last year with Spanish streamers? Small streamers, who usually have between 1-10 viewers, were gifting themselves subscriptions on their channels to save money and get the Celestial Steed Ship & Obsidian Capstan The nice scenario of supporting other creators barely existed; I myself didn't get many new subscriptions. Why is this? Because there are many people with poor money, and these campaigns don't make us more generous (unfortunately). Things are tough for Spanish-speaking creators. Sponsors aren't interested in us because we don't give money, advertisers pay us very little because we don't spend, so they don't care about us either. Our reality is quite complicated. Do you know what can provide far greater support than the Support a Streamer campaign for small or non-English-speaking creators? Having a raid run during the Orb event. Mainly because that raid brings in a huge audience of people from English-speaking countries who will stay on the stream for a good while to collect the special Drop, and during that time they'll see Twitch Ads that pay much better. Plus, it gives visibility to a channel that was probably unknown before. I assure you that this is MUCH BETTER than the Support a Streamer campaign for us. That said, I would love to see a raid for non-English-speaking creators during the Orb event, and if Rare could also give a Spanish-speaking creator the opportunity, that would be wonderful (edited)

1
LDSylvr
@𝖙𝖔𝖝𝖎𝖊 𝖘𝖎𝖓𝖈𝖑𝖆𝖎𝖗𝖊 here's the thing... I understand that being trans in a world that is still struggling to accept it must be one of, if not, the most difficult things to have to go through in life. I get it. Yesterday when I said "he", it was an honest mistake, at first. My first speech about the whole thing was just speaking my mind on the matter and I misgendered you, unintentionally because I don't know you and have had zero interactions with you before. That was my mistake and I apologize for that. I'm old and I try to keep up with as much as I can with what's going on, but some things do tend to get past me. Later in the stream, I'm looking at Twitter and I see the tweets about misgendering you, which still had totally slipped past me, and I see your post about telling me that, "you're not entitled to forgiveness", and I felt that was very disrespectful. So my response was, "you're not entitled to respect". My whole thing is this Toxie, I COMPLETELY understand how being misgendered can feel. I understand so much because it's the same feeling as being mislabeled. When I brought up bringing Hitbo's wife into the matter was wrong and she was owed an apology, you immediately attacked me and mislabeled me by stating, "there's literally clips of you saying the f slur". Why would you do that? Why would you label me by something from my past that I have moved on from? The same goes with me misgendering you. That's something from your past that you have moved on from and I wrong having done so. And again, I apologize for that. Bottom line Toxie, I'm willing to move on from this. I don't expect your forgiveness from my past, but just know that it's something I've learned from and haven't repeated. That word was something that was used in every day vocabulary from where I came from. I'm not saying that as an excuse either, but rather saying that as an accomplishment, for me, to not have repeated that mistake. To remove something so common and never repeat it again. That's a HUGE accomplishment for me that I'm very proud of... but to have thrown back in my face and mislabeling me is what upsets me, so I KNOW you KNOW exactly where I'm coming from. I'll be at SoTFest2026. I'm willing to sit down, talk, have a beer and chill out together if you are because at the end of the day, we're all EQUAL human beings, regardless of beliefs, stances and views. Nothing in this world puts someone else's value as a person higher than the person standing next to them. I hope we can come to terms on this and move past it, ma'am. You have a good day!
𝖙𝖔𝖝𝖎𝖊 𝖘𝖎𝖓𝖈𝖑𝖆𝖎𝖗𝖊 10/12/2025 8:03 PM
thank you for your apology. Before I can accept it though, your audience was very aware I'm trans and it was even pointed out to you. I would like to ask you why these types of messages were allowed to be said about me in your chat? There's also a clip of you talking about how preferred pronouns make you uncomfortable, which seems to contradict your apology. https://fxtwitter.com/hereisthetank/status/1997859859107291576?s=20
Smokey (@hereisthetank)
@ToxieSinclaire He then on a massive rant about how using preferred pronouns is outside of his comfort zone. 2 ❤️ 9 ️ 508 
FxTwitter • 8/12/2025 2:44 AM
Sayuta 10/12/2025 8:19 PM
There might have been a lack of moderation or difficulty keeping up with chat But he didn't mean to offend you It's just hard to get used to when you're older

LDSylvr 11/12/2025 12:09 AM
@𝖙𝖔𝖝𝖎𝖊 𝖘𝖎𝖓𝖈𝖑𝖆𝖎𝖗𝖊 So one thing I try to do is make sure I treat everyone as fairly and equally how they treat me. Another thing about me is that I try to educate myself as much as I can, especially with today's society, as there are so many differences. My apology above that I made, I was just going to leave it at that and move on. Whether you accepted it or slapped my hand out of the way, I was just going to leave it be. But something was really bothering me and it was the fact that I was being called transphobic for knowingly choosing not to call you by your preferred pronoun(s), after the unknowingly part happened in the beginning, of course. I honestly didn't see that as being transphobic. But since that issue didn't sit right with me, I decided to message someone very close and dear to me, who is also trans, and educate myself on the matter. She's the one who wrote up my Chat Rules and I asked her straight up if it was considered transphobic or not and I knew I would be getting the right answer because she's taught me a lot about that community. I asked her if it was okay to post our DM's as "teaching tools" for others who may have the same question(s) that I had. She agreed as long as I blocked out her identity because she's not ready to come out to the world just yet. But when she's ready, I'll be the first in line to support her. So here is our conversation:
12:09 AM
12:09 AM
12:09 AM
12:09 AM
12:09 AM
12:11 AM
So, TLDR for those interested, knowingly misgendering is a form of transphobia, so for that @𝖙𝖔𝖝𝖎𝖊 𝖘𝖎𝖓𝖈𝖑𝖆𝖎𝖗𝖊 , I apologize for misgendering you. As Sayuta stated, I'm old. Learning all these new things take time, but one thing about me though, is that I will do my homework and I will admit when I'm wrong. And in this case, I was wrong and I'm sorry for that.
1
12:12 AM
I will have a different outlook on this for sure. That being said now... the way you attacked me for calling out how it was wrong to mention Hitbo's wife, was uncalled for and still distasteful and I don't appreciate it. I am not that person anymore.
12:12 AM
And I don't wish to be labeled as such.
12:13 AM
I'm posting this here and not in DM's because I want EVERYONE in here to be educated on this matter, in case you didn't know, just like I didn't.
12:17 AM
Now back to my chat... There is a running meme that I never read chat, which for the most part is true. My Moderators usually snap people up with a quickness for saying out of pocket shit. But when the chat is moving quickly, I skip over A LOT. I also called out in the stream for people not to attack you and for people to be respectful, but I see that part wasn't included in that Tweet. The sad thing is that people always seem to post things for clicks. It's unfortunate. Lastly, I've said my piece. I've apologized. I've stated that I didn't like the say you attacked me right out the gates, but ya know what? I'm STILL willing to put it all behind us and move on. You all have a good night! ❤️

i would like to sit and think on this . but i will apologise for jumping out the gate by pointing out there are clips of you saying the f slur. i have my reasons, but i can see how frustrating it must be to try and move on from something in your past that keeps being dragged up. i hope you can also understand why someone like me would feel uncomfortable with knowing this about your past though. i appreciate that there is some form of trying to understand on your end here. i also appreciate that you asked your audience not to harass me but i did have a couple come into my chat and comment on my appearance and such (i recognise the names)- so that, along with the screenshots i’ve shown are something to consider with the audience you’ve cultivated. i would like to move on too in all honesty. enjoy the season launch tomorrow

jjgaymer☠ 14/12/2025 10:39 PM
So there was an issue on Friday where the emote and the sails were granted early. We removed them from peoples accounts about two hours later. As for how it works a bunch of you will have both emotes because you’re both a partner and a legend. However, you should only need to use the holiday hauls emote as that’s the one that grants all of the cosmetics the Legends one only does the grapple gun. As you are partners you also get the banners appear when people look through the spy glass at you. That doesn’t happen for legends. It sounds really complex, but you’ll see it’s very easy. You can give away as many items as you like. In fact, we encourage you to be very generous. As long as you don’t sell them, we’re all good. (edited)
1

Captain Falcore 19/12/2025 10:31 PM
Not going to lie, that is shady as heck. Not seen one person who did this Treasure of hope thing get any sort of interaction at all with the Treasure of Hope peeps. I know thats not the motivation at all. But every single content creator books a 2 hour time slot with them, only to find out that 2 hour time slot is just a slapped together png on their twitter with 65 followers and no effort made outside of that
10:32 PM
Again, i;m not saying that I expect this, but going to be real here, out of all the charity campaigns i've ever done, these guys are very good at just blanking you.
10:33 PM
Will not ever promote tham again.
Puff 19/12/2025 10:41 PM
Yeah the "slot" thing I have no idea what that was.
Captain Falcore 19/12/2025 10:48 PM
To be clear, this isn’t about clout chasing or personal gain. The issue is that the two hour slot was presented as something meaningful. In a response to a query, it was stated “We’ll do everything we can to make yourself and others appear in the limelight and get focus to promote the cause.” Several smaller streamers I know were genuinely excited by this, some even booking time off work to take part. Unfortunately, what that amounted to was a single Twitter post shared with an account that has a very limited reach, and nothing further. That was honestly disappointing. If the goal is to promote a charity, it raises fair questions about whether enough effort is being made to draw attention to the cause itself. I also couldn’t see any meaningful involvement from the charity in the social posts, which makes me wonder how aware they are of how this is being handled. I’m always happy to support and raise money for charity, and this feedback isn’t meant to detract from that. It’s simply honest feedback on how the process and communication felt from a participant’s perspective. Overall, communication from the Treasure of Hope team has felt unprofessional and unclear, and it created expectations for smaller creators that weren’t met. That’s been frustrating and, ultimately, quite disappointing. (edited)
Captain Falcore 19/12/2025 10:56 PM
Plain example here. Wheres the Tweet or even the hashtag for the charity involved? https://x.com/thanks4gaming/status/1999222786863112610 (edited)

We’re going live tomorrow — come see who’s joining the adventure! Donate, support, and redeem your treasures: https://t.co/72fRVRMgLY Also don't miss out Sea of Thieves' Orb Streams at https://t.co/2TJGiyXM0P starting tomorrow 7:00pm UTC - Sunday 5:00pm UTC

ShikMyPants 20/12/2025 1:08 PM
Seperate point. The community goal for 250k presents. Maybe im understanding it wrong and please correct me if so. But to give out a present a player has to be able to accept it as they havent already got it. Meaning that the maximum number of potential presents that can be reached is the maximum number of distinct players playing during the event. 95% (guesstimate) of players ive bumped into say ive already got it. And we arent close to the goal yet. Ive been unable to complete my personal challenges despite offereing a LOT of people the present. Obviously we have 13 days left so there will be a few more players increasing the numbers. But was it considered that there just wouldnt be enough people playing to actually meet the goal?

Ophie 27/12/2025 6:01 PM
Attention all! Looking for some sweaty goobers! I've been bringing some vetted GTARP folks into SoT and letting them guide the sail. They have no idea what theyre doing, but are all good people and have been loving the game, and it has been a ton of fun to experience the game for the first time through them. My goal is to set up a recurring series where I bring one vetted swabbie on a boat full of sweats, and we just sail around and let those swabbies make all of our choices for us, while we teach them about the game and help them deal with the consequences of their choices. It has been a blast, and I'm looking for some sweaty folks that want to play Protect the Swabbie President.
Ophie

LokiJimmyB 29/01/2026 7:57 AM
Sword is OP? I takes me more than 6 hits to kill somebody .. 50% of the time if the server is bad. In my 6 swings or m1 taps you killed my whole crew with a flintlock Sword is absolute broken. And not on the "way to strong side" For a good doublegunner, and i consider you one, Sword is absolute easy to manage I have no doubts thats hard to fight against multiple people with swords.. but time to kill with doublegun is so extreme high in comparison to the sword. Sword is not op. In my opinion it barely keeps up. And its a question of balance. Of course the sword have to be kind of strong to compete. Especially with all the changes recently. Crazy blunderbus knockback for example
8:01 AM
When i try to kill somebody with a sword, you can easy eat pineapples to never die. When you doublegun someone.. hes not even able to do anything against it with his food. Because it takes to much time to eat before the gunner shots his second shot (Positioning aside but that counts for the above example too) I dont want to rant about the sword, and its nothing against you @Elmuchacho of course, but i cant say nothing when i see those sword is OP comments everywhere
LokiJimmyB
When i try to kill somebody with a sword, you can easy eat pineapples to never die. When you doublegun someone.. hes not even able to do anything against it with his food. Because it takes to much time to eat before the gunner shots his second shot (Positioning aside but that counts for the above example too) I dont want to rant about the sword, and its nothing against you @Elmuchacho of course, but i cant say nothing when i see those sword is OP comments everywhere
Elmuchacho 29/01/2026 8:39 AM
If a double-gunner lands two shots, it’s a well-deserved and quick kill. If he misses his shots, he gets punished by wasting time on the reload. A sword player, on the other hand, almost never gets punished. If he misses his M1 spam (RT in your case), nothing happens. The only real punishment for a sword player is missing a sword dash—and even that can be canceled by grabbing a cannon, sail, or capstan on a ship. The most overpowered aspect of the sword is the stun when you get hit. We all know the “Swordnado” moment: once you get hit, it’s extremely hard to escape. Against double-gunners, you can also pre-eat pineapples to survive.
SlimeyPigeon 29/01/2026 8:59 AM
Sword is OP if they know how to use movement to close distance with cover Double gun is OP if they land shots Double Barrell pistol is OP if you do a blackflip, sacrifice your kidney and recite the entire Bee Movie script just so the bullets register (Double gunners think sword is op, Sword users think Double gun is op, you'll go round in circles here) (edited)
Elmuchacho
If a double-gunner lands two shots, it’s a well-deserved and quick kill. If he misses his shots, he gets punished by wasting time on the reload. A sword player, on the other hand, almost never gets punished. If he misses his M1 spam (RT in your case), nothing happens. The only real punishment for a sword player is missing a sword dash—and even that can be canceled by grabbing a cannon, sail, or capstan on a ship. The most overpowered aspect of the sword is the stun when you get hit. We all know the “Swordnado” moment: once you get hit, it’s extremely hard to escape. Against double-gunners, you can also pre-eat pineapples to survive.
LokiJimmyB 29/01/2026 12:21 PM
i dont get the "well deserved" argument to be honest. Is a well placed dash and 2 hit combo slash not a deserved kill if everthing a doublegunner (or blunderbuss user in general) has to do is hipfire me to totally destroy my move? and why are we talking about punishment? its not a punishment when you cant hit your oponent. its the same for a sword guy. if im out of range and dont hit my swing.. i deal no damage.. both can be considered "unfortunate aim". i call it that because how good you are.. everybody misses here and there so.. dont want to call it "bad aim". maybe in that moment.. or bad luck. whatever I get it. You have to reload, and the sword guy doesnt have to. But btw, there is a punishment for swords as well. When i dont hit, there is a bigger gap between my swings. 3 times 1 slash takes more time if you dont hit in comparison to a 3 hit combo where hit 1 and 2 really count and hit the enemy. And the swordnado aspect or stun.. most of the time the stun doesnt even work. dont know how other swordplayers experience it. and even if it works. the 3rd hit of a combo pushes my enemy back or away from me. so it increases my distance to him to do my final hit (if im fortunate and kill with 4 hits instead of 6) I would like if this "this or that is OP" mentality is thrown away. the sword isnt op. If you land your shots, im dead before i do anything. no matter how good i am. and no. if im hit by a doublegunner.. i cant eat fast enough to survive the second shot if it lands. 0 chance. And it cant be the solution, even if it works and you are kind of right, that i join a fight with a pineapple in my hand and pre-eat before i even got hit with the first just for the slight chance that i even get hit by your first or than survive the second. i already see the "look at that idiot coming with a pineapple to a gunfight" jokes. the time to kill for doublegunners is way shorter than for swordplayers. and if you let him get close with this insane knockback of the blunder right now.. its bad luck or just a good opponent that may have more to offer than only m1 spam (edited)

Puff 29/01/2026 12:30 PM
I've been a double gunner over sword user more than anything although I've used sword plenty for maybe last 1-2 years. There are pro's and con's with both, it all depends on the situation. Of course the sword user will be at a disadvantage if you go on an open location VS a DG. You can't use the environment to your advantage as you should. No one should also run in a straight line and not do skip jumps with a sword when fighting a DG. Attempting a sword lunge is also risky. As for any weapon combo you need to play at your advantage depending on where you are and what you're doing.
12:31 PM
As for on a boat, the sword WILL be an advantage. It's lacking room, more lag and dsyncs. this where most DG can sometimes give up DG and equip a sword to counter the spawn camp. (edited)
12:31 PM
It's about knowing what is best for the situation.

Elmuchacho 29/01/2026 12:32 PM
I think a lot of sword players forget they have a second weapon slot, because the sword itself is already a very strong standalone weapon. After hitting your opponent three times and getting a slight knockback, you can simply swap to a sniper and finish them off.

I never understood this little war between swords and dual guns in the game. Dying with a sword is like getting hit by a lightning double tap to the face. If you remove or nerf swords, you'll kill off what's left to lure players away and challenge the skilled ones at sea.

Leator 10/02/2026 7:15 PM
It had been just short of 10 minutes in the past, haven‘t checked it out since then. Any kind of direct ingame input. (Camera movement, new emote, small step, pulling out / putting away equipment) resets the timer. Any inmenu navigation, talking to npcs, browsing stores do not count as valid input

10mins makes more sense. I think you might be right there

𝕻𝖆𝖓𝖟𝖊𝖗𝖇𝖑𝖎𝖙𝖟 19/02/2026 7:58 AM
Hi everyone, I'd like to suggest some ideas for the game. I know I'm relatively new to this partnership, and I apologize if my knowledge is limited, but I think I can offer a few ideas that I find really interesting. I don't know if it's technically possible, so let me know. There's something missing from the game: pirates love games of chance. They used to gamble a lot at sea, and if we could make pirate taverns more lively with player-versus-player options, that would be fantastic. For example: you play with one or more other players and wager your gold. A roll of the dice would determine the winner and could even lead to a big brawl like in the good old days. We need a safe, random player-versus-player system to spice things up. Example #2: we could win monthly cosmetic items for ships, characters, or even random items by playing dice with our gold at the tavern keeper's. This last item doesn't really serve any other purpose than to serve drinks to the pirates. Pirates used to win ships through gambling in taverns, and getting a unique cosmetic item for your ship during a gambling session is really awesome. A bit bolder The ability to build houses defended by real players on islands. Building materials, such as weapons, walls, palisades, factories, etc., could be sold by merchants. Owning small islands would lead to huge battles for the conquest of these occupied islands, sometimes heavily fortified. Once an island is under foreign control, server merging would be impossible. Simple factories could produce resources and food (like pineapples!). Occupied islands could, for example, raise a merchant flag to trade with others and gain reputation/levels. We could also implement the "Black Spot"! A mission would allow you to mark an emissary ship on your server. Once sunk, this ship would reward you with unique cosmetic items, gold, etc. The ship randomly marked by this mission could neither dive nor merge. The mission would be timed at 15 minutes. If it isn't completed, the Black Point ship will gain gold and reputation for its emissary. I have other ideas, and again, I don't know if they're possible, but one thing is for sure: the community will like them.

Hey folks, I've been getting a lot of issues with disconnections and artifacting on twitch. I've run through all of the troubleshooting crap that the internet, reddit, google, and AI can possibly throw my way. I changed my bitrate to 5800, and scaled my resolution down to 1664x936 instead of 1080p to make it easier for the bitrate to stay stable. I'm running a 3070ti and a 9850x3d. Any suggestions from folks who have dealt with this before? (edited)
HitboTC 19/02/2026 7:43 PM
Make sure everything is updated
7:43 PM
and stop using OBS because it’s garbage and use Meld Studio
Ophie
Hey folks, I've been getting a lot of issues with disconnections and artifacting on twitch. I've run through all of the troubleshooting crap that the internet, reddit, google, and AI can possibly throw my way. I changed my bitrate to 5800, and scaled my resolution down to 1664x936 instead of 1080p to make it easier for the bitrate to stay stable. I'm running a 3070ti and a 9850x3d. Any suggestions from folks who have dealt with this before? (edited)
Rainy 19/02/2026 8:03 PM
When I was disconnecting a lot I needed to change my encoder from fast to fastest and it helped a lot. I'm assuming you did that. I also pray you are not wifi streaming. That causes its own headaches.
Captain Falcore 19/02/2026 9:37 PM
I’ll say this about any streaming software: if you bloat it too much, it will flat out fail on you. I started with OBS and added way too many scenes, many of which I only used once, and I never cleaned it up. Eventually, it just pooped itself and started acting all kinds of weird. I blamed OBS and switched to SLOBS. At first, it was great, but after a few months, the same thing happened. Now I’m back to OBS with a clean slate, and I’ve learned something important, if you make a scene for something, delete it right after and keep your setup tidy. Don’t leave stray overlays or extensions lying around. And this is especially crucial for anything that touches audio or graphics codecs. Always make sure everything is up to date, graphics cards, soundboards, BIOS, Windows updates, everything. All the time. ALL THE TIME. Don't rely on auto update, force updates atleast once a week!
3
HitboTC
and stop using OBS because it’s garbage and use Meld Studio
2oldRudi 19/02/2026 9:46 PM
I know that you like it really a lot, but is it really better ? And is the transition easy, I mean to handle the menus and everything.. ?
2oldRudi
I know that you like it really a lot, but is it really better ? And is the transition easy, I mean to handle the menus and everything.. ?
Captain Falcore 19/02/2026 9:47 PM
Meld studio is like 15% less taxing on processor, which is great for a 1 PC setup, nit sure what Hitbo uses, but if you're on a 1 PC setup Meld is 100% your go-to

Captain Falcore 19/02/2026 9:48 PM

Ophie
Hey folks, I've been getting a lot of issues with disconnections and artifacting on twitch. I've run through all of the troubleshooting crap that the internet, reddit, google, and AI can possibly throw my way. I changed my bitrate to 5800, and scaled my resolution down to 1664x936 instead of 1080p to make it easier for the bitrate to stay stable. I'm running a 3070ti and a 9850x3d. Any suggestions from folks who have dealt with this before? (edited)
Sponge 19/02/2026 9:49 PM
Personally I’ve never gone with the “stream under 1080” thing. My recommendation is, leave it at 1080p 6K bitrate, slow encoding, nvidia encoder. But maybe the 3070ti might hold u back ‍♂️ also, if you’ve got a bunch of browser source overlays, that can be bad (edited)
Captain Falcore 19/02/2026 9:51 PM
Oh my advice is, purely to make sure you don't burn out your GFX. Never go with GFX encoding. If you have a good processor, go with Software, you always get better quality. Ever wondered why when you watch your streams when lots of stuff is going on you get artifacts? Blurring of pixels. Its because you're using hardware encoding. Always use software if you have a beefy proc.
Captain Falcore
That ONLY problem with Meld is obviously the lack of plugins/extensions

1
9:55 PM
Although Twitch’s maximum bitrate is 6k, I usually recommend setting the buffer to 9k, since activity spikes can cause a slight drop in quality, essentially acting as a protective buffer, that is of course if you're ISP can handle it.
1
10:00 PM
Keep in mind that Twitch’s max is 6k, but that doesn’t mean it will always stream at 6k. If you set it to 6k and Twitch experiences issues, it might drop to 5.5k or even 4.5k. By setting your max to 9k, it will simply default to 6k when needed. This isn’t speculation, it’s how it actually works. As spoken from the silver bearded lips of the CEO.
PhuzzyBond 20/02/2026 2:59 AM
I use OBS with enhanced encoding, on youtube and twitch at the same time, basically streams are at 2K, 9K bitrate for each youtube and twitch, so total literally shows 18K, however i do have dual system, but the GPU on my stream PC is a 1650s, the encoder on that is equal to the 3090
3:00 AM
and indeed, normal encoding is 6K, when you enable enhanced it needs higher for the 2K

Captain Falcore 27/02/2026 10:57 PM
I have to say it , at this point, Mike is basically my messiah. But that line, “We smuggle things from other players,” absolutely broke me. We’ve been doing that since 2018. That’s not exactly a groundbreaking character arc, especially for a group whose entire identity is simply: pirates. And pirates steal. That’s the job description. Stealing and smuggling are not the same thing. If my wife has cookies and I snatch one straight out of her hand and eat it? That’s piracy. If she hides the cookies, I secretly take them, stash them somewhere, and plan to sell them to the highest bidder later? That’s smuggling. See? Very different crimes. Also, anyone know any good dirvorce laywers?
2
𝕻𝖆𝖓𝖟𝖊𝖗𝖇𝖑𝖎𝖙𝖟 1/03/2026 11:15 AM
We need to surprise players and encourage more interaction. The seas are deserted, except for a few battles at the start of the new global event. The Mega Stash Event works well because the loot is random, and could be formidable, which is exciting and fun. Being a pirate, whether at sea or in ports, is a risky gamble. We need a secure betting system to spice up the game. What makes games exciting? Danger, interaction, rewards, and luck. (edited)
11:16 AM
Ophie 1/03/2026 2:11 PM
I've said it before, I'll say it again, if we want more interactions, that's only like 30 percent on Rare. 70 percent is on how we choose to interact with the players we run into. More often than not people are just treating other players as loot pinatas and clips, and then wonder why they're seeing less and less players. If our first response to someone knocking on our door is to punch them in the face and the post it online for the lols, we can't be surprised when people stop knocking. Rare has made some missteps, sure, but in my opinion, that's not what's killing the game. What's killing the game is the drive to treat it like it's supposed to be PvP with occasional interactions. If we're treating it like fun interactions are the exception to the rule AND we have a platform, then that's what people who views us will think as well, which furthers the problem. Go a full session without sinking a boat. Watch how much more fun it is.
1

10:38 PM

10:41 PM

10:41 PM

NateaCuiki 1/03/2026 10:47 PM
I think the lack of activity on the servers isn't solely due to the diving. The content added in recent months has also felt quite lifeless. When it all boils down to sailing many miles to find a boss or transport something bland, grinding, and selling in a rather monotonous way, it loses its appeal. The content has lacked the element that makes you feel like you're on an adventure, whether it's deciphering puzzles, new mechanics for finding the locations of the new content, and then solving something that makes you feel like you're in a mysterious and interesting place. A new boss like the Eternal Guardian is useless if it's not introduced in an interesting way. Out of nowhere, you find its sarcophagus, when the right thing to do would have been a new quest with puzzles or some mechanic to find it, or for it to be the final boss of a new Fort (Molten Sands). The sanctuaries in the Devil's Roar region are the same; you go there with a key, open them, and that's it. It would have been appropriate to have puzzles and new mechanics to find those sanctuaries and then solve something to open the entrance... How are we supposed to feel like there are adventures and stories to experience on the seas if the new content added doesn't have that element at its core? It doesn't feel like they're telling a story. The legend of the veil itself was well executed for that reason; even getting kicked by a boar is funnier than the stuff in seasons 17 and 18
SlimeyPigeon 1/03/2026 10:51 PM
If I get killed by pumba one more time when on an island ima crash out...
2
SlimeyPigeon
If I get killed by pumba one more time when on an island ima crash out...
NateaCuiki 1/03/2026 10:51 PM
NateaCuiki 1/03/2026 11:01 PM
I don't want to sound too harsh when I talk about these things, because I know that developing games must be incredibly difficult, but I strongly believe that the content from the last few months lacks narrative; it needs to be less functional, and more narrative-driven. And it's clear that Rare intends to tell a story, but they haven't been succeeding. The title of Season 18 was clearly a tribute to Indiana Jones, but I didn't feel that the content lived up to it. There were no puzzles or anything to figure out before getting the loot—that's the problem. (edited)
SlimeyPigeon
All this diving negavtity is countered by servers feeling so much more full of life of late though. Don't know what changes you did, but don't touch it, it's worked
NateaCuiki 1/03/2026 11:08 PM
I assume Rare fixed the server population issue Drew mentioned in a video a few weeks ago. The servers were becoming empty and weren't being properly added to new crews, resulting in low player populations. If that works correctly, the servers should always feel alive, regardless of whether 100 or a million people are playing, because at the end of the day, there can only be a limited number of ships in a session. The problem arises if the server isn't being populated properly.
2oldRudi 2/03/2026 7:40 AM
I agree with Hitbo and Natea both. I think the diving mechanic does affect the way the game is being played rn, but it also a bigger, deeper issue : more and more we switch from organically created moments (fights, interactions..), to the artificially created ones. SoT is a sandbox, the decisions and the agency in player interactions are key. We lost that a bit. If you spend 8 hours a day in the game, I’m sure you can still find it.. but most of us now don’t have the energy to navigate in empty seas for hours anymore :/ Hope we will find that sandbox magic that we lost. I still remember how the Fort of the Damned carried the game for years. It was simple, and players could interact with it as they want : farm it, steal it, tuck it, avoid it.. Now we are taking you to island A, to finish boss X, and then you have yo go to island B to activate the beam for the players to find you.. let the players live the open world, organically.
Podognon 2/03/2026 8:46 AM
In my opinion diving is not really the issue : when a player can go away of the server faster with diving, he can also spawn on the server faster, with already full supplies and ready to fight. For me the problem is mostly that the events are too easy : we have not enough time to create stories if a FotD can be done in 5 minutes, and players have no need to create alliances to finish them. I would like to see a new world event very lucrative but also very difficult. People will need to do alliances to finish them faster, and they will take enough time to do it for other players to come.

The current codes on Lurkit are meant to last for a month since we launched them, and then there will be new codes to claim with the release of new Season. When Season 19 launches you will all have enough codes to claim from the new missions to last for the entire Season (edited)

H1gh Voltage 18/03/2026 12:03 PM
That's true and it's the only reason we tried to stay away from codes as much as possible in the past. The codes, however, are extremely convenient to use for everyone involved. So, the only thing we can do from our side is put expiration dates on, while you can get creative with rules and cooldowns around your own giveaways if you wish to. Still, nothing will be enough to discourage code reselling completely. They will pop up on marketplaces here and there, the only thing we can do is try to minimise the quantity ‍♀️

Spar 22/03/2026 3:33 AM
And even with precautions which we all know, they really only need actual gamertags and I know I have brought this up before (which even this wont ultimately help with being listed) the game has the tendancy to randomly disconnect you (hazelnutbeard, random anti-cheat etc). Even if you have hidden names when you reconnect it doesn't hide those names (it will show the entire crews actual gamertags). All they need is just 1 frame (and no I wasn't cheating lol, I was bringing up anchor and got booted for anti-cheat). Believe this couldnt be fixed because something microsoft related? I forget. Experienced players obviously would hide screen but it's very very easy to have a brain fart when this happens.
Db 22/03/2026 5:10 AM
You guys have to figure out a way with the spam invite to stop being a thing regarding Peanut trying sot today, the constant invite coming through with gamebar is nonsense and should really be fixed, and there has a way for you guys to being able to hide gamertags in a way where people can't server list and find the people on a server maybe a encryption or something, it wont fix ddosing but atleast it will be harder for them to know if they are on the same servers, I think you guys needs to HEAVILY rework streamer mode cause has soon that they accidently showed gamertags it was over... they knew what to look for and immediately got them off the game
Spammals 22/03/2026 6:32 AM
In total 42k people watched Peanut... • Load In • Get Supplies • Sail to a Shipwreck • DDOS attack • Team quit Crew Mate: "No matter what we do tonight this will continue to happen and there is quite literally nothing we can do about it." * Peanut: "That's a shame...* " and sounds genuinely sad. Peanut was so hyped to play. Seemingly even had stream assets made to support it. This could have been huge for both the game, promotion, positive media, sales but also Twitch viewership for everyone. Yet another horrible experience and failure. While there are options in game to mitigate issues it misses the mark. A complete overhaul to load in, menu and background services like spam prevention need to happen. As for DDOS & server listing. While I can sympathize It's a tricky issue with no silver bullet, it's been a known issue for years now and still happens with relative ease. Its simply not good enough. You only get so many chances before you get written off. The decline of SoT wont be because "It ran its course" but rather because fires were ignored until everything burned down. Invite Spam - https://www.twitch.tv/theburntpeanut/clip/ProudPunchyOkapiNerfRedBlaster-yXaRGFmDr9PDs_FR Continued Spam - https://www.twitch.tv/theburntpeanut/clip/PunchyChillyOrangeKeepo-DzpAYCpr9E9MYarD DDOS Attack - https://www.twitch.tv/theburntpeanut/clip/WimpyDifficultLlamaResidentSleeper-bl61pObJhfjMGs0t Game Over - https://www.twitch.tv/theburntpeanut/clip/VastPatientCourgetteTwitchRaid-SFGtE2r1_f1Nq26C (edited)

Ophie 22/03/2026 1:36 PM
Yeah, something needs to be done about this. MS is one of the biggest companies in the world. Surely they can improve their security to a point that you need more than a C average in high school to break through it. Rather than having 41k people considering trying the game, we're going to have 41k second guess whether or not its even worth it to try. We know (or think) Microsoft is making the security decisions, but they are consistently dropping the ball, and someone needs to stand up for the game and the players, because their apathy and complacency is doing massive damage.

Flux 22/03/2026 1:37 PM
Agreed, there's no excuses. This has to be fixed NOW
1:37 PM
One dev and a few cups of coffee can fix this
Leator 22/03/2026 1:44 PM
Lika a year ago, w hen Loki and I got spaminvited we had to change our security / privacy settings. Back then you had only one option which would also block any communication from people you aren‘t friends with. Which is a ridiculous bad setting to have. So I‘ve messaged a friend from the Xbox Hardware team, described the issue and suggested to split that option in multiple settings so you could block invites but still hear ingame communications. They passed the message on and I came back with a followup question and according to them the team would look into it, at some point, but it‘s a bigger change than I thought. Haven‘t checked if they‘ve changed it by now. But ye, could be the „easiest“ solution
Captain Falcore 22/03/2026 2:05 PM
When "Hide Gamertags" is on, it should simply just look like this as the Set Sail screen. Simple as that, job done. I realise that being a streamer you need to take steps to protect yourself, but the game literally shows up your name in bright neon lights sometimes when you're not ready for it to hide the screen. (edited)
2
2:05 PM
default for clarity.
Ophie 22/03/2026 2:14 PM
It shouldn't just be an issue of hiding gamertags though. This is a backend security issue that the UI complicates and makes the player's responsibility. Hiding your GT won't stop someone with too much time on their hands from figuring out your GT and exploiting the available back doors to keep doing the same stuff. If Rare can't fix the server vulnerabilities, then Microsoft has got to step in. Opportunities aren't being wasted, they're becoming liabilities, and that's not fair to Rare, to us, to the people giving the game a chance, and to the entire playerbase at large. This can't keep happening with every chance we're given to grow.
LDSylvr 22/03/2026 2:55 PM
The thing is this as well… his account he used last night, along with Hutch and whoever else, is cooked now. The bad actors, in j0toro’s words, now have all of their XUID’s. It doesn’t matter now if they change their GT’s or not on that account. The XUID does not change. So if they were to ever want to play again, they would have to make a whole new account. @Captain Falcore I agree with you and @Spar about the title UI. And like Spar, it was something I asked for to change because I was getting sniped doing Nightmare Mode or a PL Grind. I would get kicked off the server or died in NM Mode and have to reset my pirate and BOOM, there’s my GT on the title UI. I was told this couldn’t be changed due to MS Security reasons.
2:57 PM
But again, just to emphasize this to you all who think changing your GT will save you now… it won’t. They have every one of your XUID’s now because of Microsoft’s public API.
Captain Falcore 22/03/2026 3:03 PM

LDSylvr 23/03/2026 4:46 PM
Hitbo is showing the free version of it. There is a + version out there that shows a lot more, including:

  • Server IP
  • Server Region
  • Server Age
  • Etc

LDSylvr 26/03/2026 1:36 PM
GG’s to the Rare Development Team for trying last night. Hearing Peanut say, “We have like 10 Devs watching and ready…” tells me that you guys tried to take steps and measures to ensure their session was a safe and pleasant one. Unfortunately, you just can’t prevent incompetence and when streamers think they know it all and ignore clear instructions and guidance (ie. create NEW accounts), and they just name change instead, keeping the same XUID like dummies, there is nothing you guys could have done to stop what had transpired. So I’ll go ahead and say it since everyone else seems to be bitching, but I appreciate you guys trying. Really does say a lot. (edited)

LDSylvr 26/03/2026 1:43 PM
100% … there’s a lot of work that needs to be done to eliminate all these security risks for sure. Unfortunately, events like this sometimes have to happen to light a fire under some asses (MS in this case), in order to see it. But, if what Peanut said last night was true and Devs were there to help, that should tell everyone, “Look guys! This is not something that Rare can fix solely by themselves. But they are here trying and doing their best to prevent it and help out where needed.”… I respect that. It tells me that they’re not just throwing him (Peanut) to the wolves. And keep in mind that whatever game Peanut plays that’s PvP, he’s getting sniped all the time. Every single game. Back in the day, that’s the issue we had to deal with. But now, they know how to crash a server.

I’m frustrated for Rare. Imagine running a machine, but the machine always leaks oil. You’ve discovered and identified where the leak is coming from, but you need Corporate to approve the maintenance to fix it. Corporate says, “Just keep putting pads down to absorb the leak. Morning guys! Re: the streamer mode, there is nothing new in it. What we're looking to do is consolidate the existing settings into one 'creator/streaming' menu so that people coming into our game from outside the ecosystem, who don't know about these settings, can more easily find them. We are also looking if we can do one 'one button to rule them all' where you hit it and it toggles all appropriate settings on, but in terms of those settings there is nothing new. We are looking into the hide tags on setting sail but that's a platform level compliance thing so may not be possible. (edited)

2
🚫Back on Wednesday🚫
Morning guys! Re: the streamer mode, there is nothing new in it. What we're looking to do is consolidate the existing settings into one 'creator/streaming' menu so that people coming into our game from outside the ecosystem, who don't know about these settings, can more easily find them. We are also looking if we can do one 'one button to rule them all' where you hit it and it toggles all appropriate settings on, but in terms of those settings there is nothing new. We are looking into the hide tags on setting sail but that's a platform level compliance thing so may not be possible. (edited)

🚫Back on Wednesday🚫
Morning guys! Re: the streamer mode, there is nothing new in it. What we're looking to do is consolidate the existing settings into one 'creator/streaming' menu so that people coming into our game from outside the ecosystem, who don't know about these settings, can more easily find them. We are also looking if we can do one 'one button to rule them all' where you hit it and it toggles all appropriate settings on, but in terms of those settings there is nothing new. We are looking into the hide tags on setting sail but that's a platform level compliance thing so may not be possible. (edited)
MonkyJV 🇫🇷 29/03/2026 11:38 AM
Oh. Too bad. A button to toggle streamer mode on and off would be useful, yes, even though I’ve been doing it for years using a shortcut set up in the keybindings. It’s useful for reports to display usernames. What would be good to add for streamer mode is the ability to replace GamerTags in the game launch menu, when launching a ship and when a mate has been disconnected, because in the crew menu (Esc) they remain visible for 15 minutes or until they reconnect, even with streamer mode enabled.
𝕻𝖆𝖓𝖟𝖊𝖗𝖇𝖑𝖎𝖙𝖟 29/03/2026 11:39 AM
*when a mate has been disconnected This has been also a problem with Peanuts, and need to be fixed if possible.
1
MonkyJV 🇫🇷
Oh. Too bad. A button to toggle streamer mode on and off would be useful, yes, even though I’ve been doing it for years using a shortcut set up in the keybindings. It’s useful for reports to display usernames. What would be good to add for streamer mode is the ability to replace GamerTags in the game launch menu, when launching a ship and when a mate has been disconnected, because in the crew menu (Esc) they remain visible for 15 minutes or until they reconnect, even with streamer mode enabled.

World of Chap 30/03/2026 5:57 PM
So... I recognize that we've got season 20 releasing shortly after the big xbox showcase this year, and I'm sure we're trying to avoid having a lackluster reveal like the smugglers... But, framing that direct as if there is nothing else coming in Season 20 other than custom servers was a strange choice. If that is the case, that means we aren't getting any new adventure content for 4 months straight minimum. So the question now is, did GMU get pushed back to season 21 and the amount of resources required to make that big update happen caused a huge delay, forcing custom servers to be the content for a whole season? Or is it just being held back for a big hype reveal... Imo, a community direct is a good enough place to at least mention that more is coming if that's the case. I still find it odd that the act 2 of season 19 is just a weekend long event. There simply has to be something else here, right? It could be effective. Surprise everyone at the xbox showcase with the big reveal and such. But that could be achieved without leaving us wondering if new content is coming at all

Captain Falcore 30/03/2026 8:44 PM
Calling it hating is a bit much. It’s just people pointing out where things didnt match what was said, which is fair. Custom Servers are great, I actually love that they’re coming, but that’s not really the issue here The problem is what was promised vs what we got. Across dev directs, updates and podcasts they said this one would cover a lot more, even Insider changes, and that just didnt happen We were told this dev direct would include
  • More transparency about the future
  • More on possible new ship types
  • Expansion on what Pirate Legend means
  • Changes to Insiders
  • A look at Season 20 and what’s next
  • A clearer idea of the update cadence
None of that really got covered. Instead it was mostly stuff we already knew, and even some details were off like saying Friday instead of Wednesday (for LSS testing) So yeah, it’s not about hating the update, it’s just frustration that a Community Direct wasnt really that direct. Honestly felt like it could of been an email (edited)

LDSylvr 31/03/2026 4:07 AM
I’m in agreement with @𝖙𝖔𝖝𝖎𝖊 𝖘𝖎𝖓𝖈𝖑𝖆𝖎𝖗𝖊 … Custom Servers were a thing that the majority of us were waiting for and when it was announced that it was going to be a paid subscription, that put a lot of people off that were excited about it. With the announcement of it being free now, that’s good news to a lot of people. With S20, people now have the option to actually create their own adventures and events. Those choosing not to can continue on with High Seas. I seriously doubt the majority of the community is 100% complete with their commendations. There are still things to do. I get that some people want something “new”… something new to always do. As Content Creators, we have the talent and the ability to keep our audience entertained. Let’s be real here… there is always something to do in this game. And now that server migration and refilling has been “fixed”, there are ships all over. Another thing, as Partners, we are representatives and ambassadors of this game. We are an extension of Rare and it is our job, since we are on the front lines, to deal with the criticisms and handle them… not to incite it and join in with it. That gets us nowhere and just continues to fan the flame. It’s okay to have disagreements with Rare’s decisions or content releases, but it’s your job as a Partner and representative of the game to find the good in it as well. Most of you know that I speak my mind openly about the game and the decisions the Devs make, but at the same time, I always shine a light on the matter in another way and point out the good things I can still do in this game that people still enjoy watching. Just a couple of ideas of things to do that people love watching from my experience that you don’t need new content to pull off:
  • Loot Stacking until you get sunk. Even better if you can make it the whole session without getting sunk and giving the loot away to viewer(s)
  • Nightmare Mode (still hasn’t been beaten by a solo)
  • Different challenges of leveling a new pirate to Pirate Legend
  • Holding Giveaway Events for your communities and inviting them to compete with one another on stream
  • etc etc… the possibilities are endless and the point is, you don’t always have to make content from shitting on random players Bottom line, I personally think S20’s custom servers will be enough to last the entire season and give Rare time to focus more on what’s to come with S21. Just remember guys, there’s more to this game than just sinking ships.
 (edited)
𝖙𝖔𝖝𝖎𝖊 𝖘𝖎𝖓𝖈𝖑𝖆𝖎𝖗𝖊
we’ve been here before and things are fine. like a lot us are still full time. with communities who love watching the game . why are we dooming because next season isn’t bringing adventure content , when most of you have been hating on the adventure content since season 11. like you don’t even enjoy the adventure content they make atp anyway
Spammals 31/03/2026 8:58 AM
For what it’s worth I’ve lost my job and can’t stream full time anymore. But I’m gonna own that failure and not blame anything but myself. (edited)
PhuzzyBond 31/03/2026 9:13 AM
SoT will do well if Custom Servers just keep getting features, and main game gets stability and bug fixes. The only reason Minecraft survived that long is because of the user created content, the only reason GTA survived that long is because of the RP content. So hopefully, the custom servers will be on the same path. Not undermining the content Rare provided in SoT through the years, but i do not think anything can beat user generated content in games, as that does not require deadlines, board meetings or a budget, so possibilities are endless. My only, "possibly far future request" is custom server API
Captain Falcore 31/03/2026 11:21 AM
Yeah, all grievances aside, i'm super pumped for Custom Servers, have been for 6 years

1
LDSylvr
I’m in agreement with @𝖙𝖔𝖝𝖎𝖊 𝖘𝖎𝖓𝖈𝖑𝖆𝖎𝖗𝖊 … Custom Servers were a thing that the majority of us were waiting for and when it was announced that it was going to be a paid subscription, that put a lot of people off that were excited about it. With the announcement of it being free now, that’s good news to a lot of people. With S20, people now have the option to actually create their own adventures and events. Those choosing not to can continue on with High Seas. I seriously doubt the majority of the community is 100% complete with their commendations. There are still things to do. I get that some people want something “new”… something new to always do. As Content Creators, we have the talent and the ability to keep our audience entertained. Let’s be real here… there is always something to do in this game. And now that server migration and refilling has been “fixed”, there are ships all over. Another thing, as Partners, we are representatives and ambassadors of this game. We are an extension of Rare and it is our job, since we are on the front lines, to deal with the criticisms and handle them… not to incite it and join in with it. That gets us nowhere and just continues to fan the flame. It’s okay to have disagreements with Rare’s decisions or content releases, but it’s your job as a Partner and representative of the game to find the good in it as well. Most of you know that I speak my mind openly about the game and the decisions the Devs make, but at the same time, I always shine a light on the matter in another way and point out the good things I can still do in this game that people still enjoy watching. Just a couple of ideas of things to do that people love watching from my experience that you don’t need new content to pull off:
  • Loot Stacking until you get sunk. Even better if you can make it the whole session without getting sunk and giving the loot away to viewer(s)
  • Nightmare Mode (still hasn’t been beaten by a solo)
  • Different challenges of leveling a new pirate to Pirate Legend
  • Holding Giveaway Events for your communities and inviting them to compete with one another on stream
  • etc etc… the possibilities are endless and the point is, you don’t always have to make content from shitting on random players Bottom line, I personally think S20’s custom servers will be enough to last the entire season and give Rare time to focus more on what’s to come with S21. Just remember guys, there’s more to this game than just sinking ships.
 (edited)

Sponge 4/04/2026 9:23 PM
This is leftover from mast bug. If you look at rigging, you can see that the ropes are at the incorrect weird angle, and their side windows will be rendered shut (even if they aren’t) until the enemy team interacts with them while you are nearby. When mast bug was “fixed” the only things fixed were the mast and the anchor, (and maybe the wheel I can’t remember), and also the issue doesn’t occur on your own boat anymore, (you weren’t able to load yourself into your own cannons) leftover was the rigging, windows, cannons not loading on the opponents boat sometimes, and the ammo box Rare pls fix
Exported 3,048 message(s)
Timezone: UTC+0
